title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-ADOPTION-CI — Define usable-compiler quality gate for parallel Core work
description: Create the gate that decides when WPHX PHP is usable enough for normal WordPress Core port slices to resume in parallel. The gate must measure generated PHP quality, unsupported constructs, raw-block/template debt, public ABI shape, bootstrap/runtime evidence, source/debug behavior, and known fallbacks so the project does not refactor slop later.
acceptance_criteria: A documented and executable check summarizes WPHX PHP usability: required compiler fixtures pass, unsupported=[] for claimed boundaries, no inline public raw PHP bodies, no active unproven adapter templates, public-shell snapshots pass, runtime/std/bootstrap probes pass for selected pilots, and remaining stock Haxe PHP fallback surfaces are bounded with owners. Progress matrix records when the gate is not yet passed or when it unblocks parallel Core port work.
close_reason: Adoption CI gate passed via npm run wphx:php:adoption-ci and :check; manifest/receipt record generated PHP quality, unsupported=[], raw/template debt clear, public snapshots, runtime/std/bootstrap/debug probes, and bounded stock fallbacks.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-WHOLE-FILE-PILOT — Emit a small WordPress file without stock public shape fallback
description: Select a small but real WordPress public PHP file or tightly bounded file group and emit it through WPHX PHP as original-path PHP from typed Haxe/metadata without relying on stock Haxe PHP for the public file shape or copied upstream shell bodies. The pilot should force direct-script/file-scope semantics, include timing, globals, conditional declarations, references, warnings, and generated-code readability where applicable.
acceptance_criteria: A candidate original-path WordPress file is generated by WPHX PHP, linted, snapshot-checked, ABI/reflection checked where relevant, and compared with the WordPress 7.0 oracle. Ownership manifests distinguish Haxe-owned semantics, PHP/public-boundary behavior, fallbacks, and non-claims; docs define whether the pilot unblocks broader Core work.
close_reason: Added WPHX PHP whole-file pilot for the real WordPress wp-includes/class-http.php file with no stock public fallback, no Haxe bootstrap, whole_file_owned segment-plan evidence, oracle/candidate behavior comparison, public-shell snapshot coverage, and refreshed compiler gap/docs receipts.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-CORE-LOWERING-PILOT — Move repeated adapter constructs into reusable PHP core IR
description: Promote repeated public-adapter constructs from WordPress-profile-specific emitters into reusable WPHX PHP core IR and printer support. Focus on constructs that multiple WordPress slices already need or that block whole-file implementation pilots: functions, methods, properties, native arrays, references, conditionals, loops, calls, object construction, exceptions, closures, static locals, dynamic class/member access, and namespace/use emission.
acceptance_criteria: Focused compiler fixtures prove selected generic lowering features with php -l, generated-shape snapshots, manifest unsupported=[], runtime behavior probes, and docs. WordPress-profile adapters shrink or cite explicit ABI reasons for any remaining profile-specific logic.
close_reason: Added no-helper WPHX PHP typed statement lowering pilot for if/while/break/continue with manifest, receipt, runtime probe, and public-shell snapshot coverage.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-RUNTIME-STDLIB-STRATEGY — Borrow or adapt Haxe PHP runtime/std behavior
description: Define and prove the minimum runtime/std/php strategy WPHX PHP needs before it can replace stock Haxe PHP for selected private implementation output. Use ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/haxe as the oracle for boot, std/php intrinsics, arrays, strings, exceptions, reflection basics, iterators, closures, and target helpers where practical instead of reimplementing them from scratch.
acceptance_criteria: ADR/docs and receipts record the runtime/std ownership strategy, what is borrowed/adapted from stock Haxe PHP, what remains a fallback, and the first executable probes for boot, exceptions, string/array helpers, closures or iterators, and source/debug behavior needed by WordPress compiler pilots.
close_reason: Accepted ADR-017, added deterministic WPHX PHP runtime/std strategy runner, recorded stock Haxe PHP release/debug probes for boot, arrays/maps/iterators, closures, exceptions, strings/JSON, and source/debug behavior, and refreshed compiler gap inventory/docs.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-GAP-INVENTORY — Audit stock PHP target dependencies and WPHX PHP gaps
description: Inventory every place current WPHX PHP fixtures still depend on stock Haxe PHP output, copied oracle PHP, runner patches, or WordPress-profile-only adapter logic. Classify each dependency as reusable PHP core IR, WordPress profile ABI constraint, std/php runtime behavior to borrow, temporary fallback, or full-backend pressure.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable manifest and receipt list stock-target dependencies, copied/runner-patched surfaces, active profile-specific adapters, generic PHP lowering gaps, runtime/std/bootstrap dependencies, and follow-up owners; progress matrix and compiler docs identify which gaps block WPHX PHP becoming the primary compiler path.
close_reason: Recorded deterministic WPHX PHP compiler gap inventory manifest and receipt; documented counts, classifications, and remaining usable-compiler gates.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-USABLE — Make WPHX PHP usable as primary WordPress PHP compiler
description: Compiler-focus epic for making the in-repo Reflaxe-based WPHX PHP compiler usable as the primary WordPress PHP emission path. The goal is to stop accumulating stock-Haxe-PHP-shaped compromises, move public ABI and compiler-shape gaps into WPHX PHP, and keep stock Haxe PHP only as a bounded private-output/stdphp oracle until WPHX PHP can own more implementation output. This epic does not claim full arbitrary-Haxe backend maturity until the child gates pass.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX PHP has audited stock-target dependencies, reusable PHP core lowering gates, runtime/std/bootstrap strategy, at least one whole-file or implementation-output pilot that does not rely on stock Haxe PHP public shape, public generated-PHP quality gates, and documented criteria for when Core port work can resume in parallel.
close_reason: All five WPHX-COMP-PHP-USABLE child gates passed. Adoption CI records the bounded usable-compiler decision and unblocks parallel Core slices that route new public PHP pressure through WPHX PHP while keeping stock Haxe PHP as private-output/stdphp oracle.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-ADOPTION-TRACK — Adopt WPHX PHP as primary compiler focus
description: Record the strategy shift requested on 2026-07-01: move true PHP output gaps and public ABI/compiler-shape pressure into WPHX PHP now, make WPHX PHP usable as the primary WordPress PHP emission path before broadening Core port work, and keep stock Haxe PHP only as a temporary private-output/stdphp oracle while the custom compiler matures. This is an adoption/focus decision, not a claim that WPHX PHP is already a full arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
acceptance_criteria: ADR/docs/agent instructions/progress matrix record WPHX PHP as the primary focus for new WordPress public PHP emission and compiler-shape gaps; usable-compiler follow-up work is seeded in Beads; stock Haxe PHP fallback and std/php oracle role are bounded; receipts/validation pass; Beads and Git are pushed.
close_reason: Accepted ADR-016 and seeded WPHX-COMP-PHP-USABLE as the compiler-usability focus. Docs, AGENTS, oracle prompt/index, progress matrix, and receipt record that WPHX PHP is now the near-term primary focus for public WordPress PHP emission and compiler-shape gaps while stock Haxe PHP remains a bounded private-output/stdphp oracle.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-BACKEND-STRATEGY-ADR — Decide WPHX PHP adapter lane vs full Reflaxe target path
description: The in-repo WPHX PHP emitter uses Reflaxe infrastructure, but current governing docs deliberately limit its admitted scope to staged WordPress original-path public adapter emission. Recent WP_Http work retired raw/template bodies into structured IR, raising a strategic question: continue bounded adapter-IR growth, promote WPHX PHP into a fuller Reflaxe/custom PHP target, improve stock Haxe PHP, or split responsibilities. This issue exists to make that decision explicit with evidence rather than letting adapter accretion define the architecture by accident.
acceptance_criteria: An ADR or equivalent decision record compares the staged adapter lane, broader WPHX PHP backend, extracted reflaxe.php target, and stock Haxe PHP improvement paths; defines generated PHP quality gates for public WordPress distribution code; sets measurable pivot criteria for backend promotion; records risks around stdlib/runtime reuse, source maps, reflection, native arrays, mixed templates, and ecosystem compatibility; includes or explicitly defers an oracle review prompt/context bundle; updates AGENTS.md, docs/operations/wphx-php-compiler.md, docs/operations/progress-matrix.md, and Beads if the strategy changes.
close_reason: Accepted ADR-015 for WPHX PHP backend strategy, added oracle review prompt bundle, recorded operations receipt, and updated AGENTS/compiler/progress guidance.

title: Promote WP_Http request nonblocking adapter to WPHX PHP IR
description:
Why this issue exists: The final active WordPress-profile adapter template is the bounded WP_Http::request nonblocking shell body. It is compiler-owned with provenance, but it remains a raw PHP template and keeps PhpRawBlock(rendered.code) alive in the WordPress adapter profile.

What needs to be done: Add only the generic PHP IR/printer support needed for the bounded request adapter, especially try/catch, rewrite wp-http-request-nonblocking as structured WPHX PHP IR, remove the request nonblocking template, update the WPHX-312.65 runner to require template absence and IR feature evidence, refresh public-shell snapshots/raw-block policy/receipts/docs/progress matrix, and keep the deterministic nonblocking oracle/candidate gate green.

Acceptance criteria: WordPress-profile PhpRawBlock/template count drops to zero; WPHX-312.65 nonblocking candidate still passes; public-shell snapshots and adapter raw-block policy pass; receipts and Beads validate; docs/progress matrix record that no active WordPress adapter templates remain while non-claims around full WP_Http::request ownership stay explicit.
design: Keep this as bounded Adapter IR, not arbitrary PHP backend scope and not full WP_Http::request ownership. Preserve native PHP public-boundary behavior and existing nonblocking Haxe helper delegation. Do not introduce replacement templates, JS patches, or handwritten generated PHP.
close_reason: Promoted the bounded WP_Http::request nonblocking adapter from the final compiler-owned PHP template to structured WPHX PHP core IR, added try/catch and PHP constant IR support, removed the request template, refreshed WPHX-312.65 runner/snapshots/raw-block policy/receipts/docs, and kept the oracle/candidate and guard checks green with zero WordPress-profile raw blocks/templates.

title: Promote WP_Http_Cookie constructor adapter to WPHX PHP IR
description:
Why this issue exists: The WP_Http_Cookie::__construct public adapter still uses a compiler-owned PHP template. That is better than inline raw PHP, but it remains a raw adapter body instead of structured WPHX PHP IR.

What needs to be done: Add only the generic PHP IR/printer constructs needed for the bounded constructor boundary, rewrite the wp-http-cookie-construct adapter plan to structured IR, remove the cookie constructor template, update the WPHX-312.53 runner/guards/receipts/docs/progress matrix, and keep the cookie oracle/candidate and public shell checks green.

Acceptance criteria: Active WordPress adapter templates drop to the request nonblocking boundary only; WPHX-312.53 still passes all cookie oracle/candidate cases; public shell snapshots and raw-block policy pass; receipts validate; docs and progress matrix record the promotion and non-claims.
design: Keep this as WPHX PHP core IR growth, not a WordPress-only string move. Add reusable constructs such as null coalesce, ternary/list assignment, and dynamic object property only where needed. Do not introduce temporary JS-patched or handwritten PHP bodies.
close_reason: Promoted WP_Http_Cookie::__construct from compiler-owned adapter template to structured WPHX PHP core IR, removed the template, refreshed WPHX-312.53 runner/guards/snapshots/receipts/docs, and kept cookie oracle/candidate parity plus raw-block/public-shell checks green.

title: Replace large inline PHP adapter raw block
description: Generalize the WP_Http::request raw-block lesson into AGENTS.md and replace the large inline PhpRawBlock in WphxPhpWordPressAdapters with a durable compiler-owned adapter template/provenance mechanism. This must not be a temporary workaround: large PHP public adapter bodies should be explicit source artifacts with shape/provenance checks and gates, not opaque Haxe string concatenation.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md contains a generalized rule forbidding large inline raw public adapter bodies without a compiler-owned template or structured IR path; WPHX PHP request nonblocking adapter no longer contains the giant inline concatenated PHP body; compiler manifests or runner evidence record the template/provenance; WPHX-312.65 candidate, public-shell snapshots, escape-hatch audit, receipts validation, Beads validation, and precommit pass.
close_reason: Large WP_Http::request adapter body now uses a compiler-owned template with manifest provenance; AGENTS rule added; WPHX-312.65 runner requires template path/hash/placeholder/upstream evidence; gates pass.

title: Retire WP_Http request nonblocking temp shell
description: Replace the WPHX-312.65 copied/JS-patched WP_Http::request nonblocking candidate shell with a WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path class-wp-http.php adapter. Scope is bounded to the nonblocking branch evidence; do not claim full WP_Http::request ownership. Update manifests, receipts, docs, snapshots, and gates.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX PHP emits wp-includes/class-wp-http.php for the WPHX-312.65 nonblocking request candidate; runner consumes generated shell instead of patching copied PHP; manifest records empty unsupported list for claimed boundary; candidate and public-shell snapshot checks pass; docs/progress/receipts record bounded ownership and nonclaims.
close_reason: WPHX PHP now emits the bounded original-path class-wp-http.php request nonblocking shell; WPHX-312.65 runner uses the generated shell; candidate/snapshot/validation gates pass.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-RETIRE-WP-HTTP-TRANSPORT-SELECTION-TEMP-SHELL — Use generated shell in WPHX-312.66
description: Retire the temporary public PHP candidate shell for the WPHX-312.66 deprecated WP_Http transport-selection candidate. Add WPHX PHP original-path class-wp-http.php emission for a bounded WP_Http adapter that preserves public _get_first_available_transport ABI and private _dispatch_request reflection/dispatch behavior, delegates only deprecated transport token/class-name mapping to Haxe, and leaves filters, static transport test calls, transport caching, WP_Error construction, http_api_debug, http_response, and fake transport request execution in PHP. Non-goals: WP_Http::request ownership, live cURL/streams/Requests transport execution, installed distribution behavior, full transport dispatch ownership, or whole-file WP_Http ownership.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX PHP emits wp-includes/class-wp-http.php for class WP_Http with unsupported=[] and reflection-visible private _dispatch_request; WPHX-312.66 runner consumes the compiler-emitted shell without copy/patch method replacement; existing six transport selection/dispatch oracle-candidate cases pass; public-shell snapshots cover the generated class shape and private method; ownership manifests/receipts/docs/progress matrix are updated; format, focused gates, receipts, Beads validation, precommit, Dolt push, and git push pass.
close_reason: WPHX PHP now emits a bounded original-path WP_Http transport-selection shell; WPHX-312.66 consumes that generated shell and no longer copies/patches a temporary public PHP class shell. Transport candidate, public-shell snapshot, format, and escape-hatch gates pass.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-RETIRE-WP-HTTP-COOKIE-TEMP-SHELL — Use generated shell in WPHX-312.53
description: Retire the temporary public PHP candidate shell for WP_Http_Cookie. Add a WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path class shell for wp-includes/class-wp-http-cookie.php that preserves constructor parsing/defaults, public properties, AllowDynamicProperties, parse_url/time/apply_filters boundaries, and delegates the already-promoted post-construction matching/header/attribute behavior to Haxe. Update WPHX-312.53 to consume the generated shell instead of copying/patching upstream PHP. Non-goals: full constructor ownership, Requests cookie jar semantics, live HTTP transport, installed distribution behavior, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, or whole-file HTTP ownership.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX PHP emits wp-includes/class-wp-http-cookie.php for class WP_Http_Cookie with unsupported=[]; WPHX-312.53 runner consumes the compiler-emitted shell without copy/patch method replacement; existing six cookie oracle/candidate cases pass; public-shell snapshots cover the generated class shape; ownership manifests/receipts/docs/progress matrix are updated; format, focused gates, receipts, Beads validation, precommit, Dolt push, and git push pass.
close_reason: WPHX PHP now emits the original-path WP_Http_Cookie class shell; WPHX-312.53 consumes that generated shell and no longer copies/patches a temporary public PHP class shell. Cookie candidate, public-shell snapshot, format, and escape-hatch gates pass.

title: Group WP_Http parser/header/cookie adapters in one generated shell
description:
Why this issue exists: WPHX PHP now has staged custom compiler status and separate generated original-path evidence for WP_Http parser helpers, buildCookieHeader, and processHeaders. The next compiler-pressure step should prove these neighboring public/protected methods can coexist in one generated class-wp-http.php adapter before attempting broader WP_Http::request ownership or mixed template work.

What needs to be done: Add a bounded WPHX PHP driver that emits one original-path wp-includes/class-wp-http.php adapter containing WP_Http::processResponse, WP_Http::chunkTransferDecode, protected WP_Http::parse_url, WP_Http::buildCookieHeader( &$r ), and WP_Http::processHeaders( $headers, $url = '' ). Reuse existing typed Haxe helpers, native-array/core IR nodes, and oracle/candidate probes where practical. Record a manifest, ownership/receipt evidence, package scripts, and progress docs.

Acceptance criteria: The grouped generated class-wp-http.php passes php -l; reflection verifies method visibility/signatures including buildCookieHeader by-reference and protected parse_url; runtime probes cover processResponse, chunkTransferDecode, parse_url subclass access, buildCookieHeader caller native-array mutation/cookie order, and processHeaders duplicate headers/Set-Cookie/final response shape; manifest records class:WP_Http with unsupported=[]; existing focused WPHX PHP helper checks remain green; whole-file WP_Http and WP_Http::request ownership remain explicitly unclaimed.
notes: Closure note correction: Added grouped WPHX PHP original-path WP_Http helper shell emitting WP_Http::processResponse, WP_Http::chunkTransferDecode, protected WP_Http::parse_url, WP_Http::buildCookieHeader( &$r ), and WP_Http::processHeaders( $headers, $url = "" ) in one generated wp-includes/class-wp-http.php class shell, with PHP lint, reflection/behavior probes, manifest/ownership/receipt evidence, and non-claims for whole-file WP_Http, WP_Http::request, live HTTP transport, installed behavior, generated WP_Http_Cookie, and mixed-template ownership.
close_reason: Added grouped WPHX PHP original-path WP_Http helper shell emitting processResponse, chunkTransferDecode, protected parse_url, buildCookieHeader(&), and processHeaders(, ='') in one generated class-wp-http.php, with PHP lint, reflection/behavior probes, manifest/ownership/receipt evidence, and non-claims for whole-file WP_Http, WP_Http::request, live HTTP transport, installed behavior, generated WP_Http_Cookie, and mixed-template ownership.

title: Promote WPHX PHP native-array pressure into reusable PHP-core IR
description:
Why this issue exists: WPHX-COMP-PHP.06 proves WP_Http::buildCookieHeader can be generated, but the current method body is a WordPress profile pressure adapter. The custom PHP compiler should grow as a modern reusable Reflaxe PHP compiler core with WordPress profile constraints, not as a collection of WP-only body printers.

What needs to be done: Replace or reduce profile-specific native-array method bodies by adding reusable PHP-core Adapter IR nodes/lowering for foreach key/value loops, if/empty/is_object checks, native array reads and writes, array casts, object construction, method calls, assignment, and idiomatic PHP formatting. Use the native Haxe PHP generator/std/php source as a borrowable implementation oracle where useful, then apply the nodes to buildCookieHeader and prepare processHeaders.

Acceptance criteria: A minimized fixture emits these constructs through reusable PHP-core IR, not a WP-specific body template; the buildCookieHeader generated shell still passes; WPHX-COMP-PHP.07 processHeaders can depend on these nodes; docs/ADR notes preserve the modern PHP core plus WordPress profile boundary.
close_reason: Promoted buildCookieHeader native-array pressure into reusable WPHX PHP core IR/printer nodes, added manifest feature checks, docs, and compiler-core receipt.

title: Generate WP_Http::processHeaders with native header/cookie arrays
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle identified WP_Http::processHeaders as the durable promotion target after the grouped parser helpers. The current candidate evidence is useful, but durable ownership needs WPHX-emitted public shell behavior rather than copied or JS-patched PHP bodies.

What needs to be done: Extend the WPHX PHP emitter or typed adapter plan enough to emit the WP_Http::processHeaders public shell while preserving PHP-native arrays, duplicate header accumulation, Set-Cookie conversion to WP_Http_Cookie, final response block selection, return shape, and falsey/empty behavior. Haxe helpers may own scalar line decisions where they do not disturb PHP ABI semantics.

Acceptance criteria: Generated original-path class-wp-http.php includes WP_Http::processHeaders; oracle/candidate probes cover folded headers, duplicate headers, Set-Cookie conversion, final response block selection, return shape, warning/empty/falsey behavior where applicable; manifest unsupported=[]; grouped parser helper checks remain green; whole-file WP_Http ownership remains explicitly unclaimed.
close_reason: Generated WP_Http::processHeaders through WPHX PHP original-path public shell emission. The driver verifies PHP lint, reflection ABI, final response block selection, folded-header unfolding, duplicate header arrays, Set-Cookie conversion, empty/falsey return shape, manifest unsupported=[], and existing grouped parser-helper checks.

title: Generate WP_Http::buildCookieHeader with native array mutation
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle identified WP_Http::buildCookieHeader( &$r ) as the next important pressure after by-reference parameter ABI because it combines caller native-array mutation, scalar cookie upgrade, object preservation, filter timing, and generated original-path shell durability.

What needs to be done: Extend the WPHX PHP emitter or typed adapter plan with the smallest generic native PHP array mutation/default-shape support needed to emit the public WP_Http::buildCookieHeader shell, while delegating reusable scalar/header decisions to Haxe helpers where safe.

Acceptance criteria: The generated original-path class-wp-http.php shell includes WP_Http::buildCookieHeader( &$r ); PHP reflection verifies by-reference ABI; runtime probes verify mutation of the caller native PHP array, scalar-to-WP_Http_Cookie upgrade, object preservation, filter payload/timing, final Cookie header order, manifest unsupported=[]; existing grouped parser-helper checks remain green; docs/receipts preserve whole-file WP_Http non-claims.
close_reason: Generated WP_Http::buildCookieHeader(&) through WPHX PHP original-path public shell emission. The driver verifies PHP lint, reflection by-reference ABI, caller native-array mutation, scalar-to-WP_Http_Cookie upgrade, object preservation, wp_http_cookie_value filter payload/timing, final Cookie header order, manifest unsupported=[], existing grouped parser-helper checks, docs, and a compiler receipt. Also filed WPHX-COMP-PHP-CORE-IR-NATIVE-ARRAYS to promote this profile pressure into reusable PHP-core IR before processHeaders.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP.02 — Replace WPHX-312.61 chunkTransferDecode shell with compiler output
description: Use the in-repo WPHX PHP Reflaxe emitter to generate the original-path public PHP for the small WP_Http::chunkTransferDecode driver, replacing the current JS-authored candidate shell shape before attempting the full WP_Http::request method.
acceptance_criteria: The WPHX-312.61 chunkTransferDecode candidate runs against compiler-emitted wp-includes/class-wp-http.php or records a minimized compiler-pressure blocker; PHP lint and oracle/candidate probes pass; manifests and progress notes distinguish compiler-emitted public PHP from temporary shell scaffolding.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-COMP-PHP.02: WPHX-312.61 now runs against compiler-emitted build/wp-core/wphx-312-61/generated/wp-includes/class-wp-http.php for WP_Http::chunkTransferDecode. npm run wphx:php:wp-http-chunk-transfer-decode:check passes PHP lint, the existing oracle/candidate chunk-transfer probe, and WPHX PHP manifest validation for class:WP_Http with no unsupported constructs. Whole-file WP_Http ownership remains future work.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP.01 — Drive F1/F4 facade fixtures through wphx-php
description: Use the in-repo WPHX PHP Reflaxe emitter to generate public PHP for the existing F1 global-function and F4 public-class facade fixtures. This should replace JS-authored shell generation for those fixture paths where the compiler can express the public ABI, and capture compiler-pressure evidence for any missing reference/reflection behavior.
acceptance_criteria: F1 and F4 have wphx-php variants or clearly named compiler-pressure blockers; generated PHP passes existing reflection and behavior probes; manifests record emitted paths/declarations; package scripts expose check mode.
notes: 2026-06-29: F1 compiler driver checkpoint landed locally. The wphx-php emitter now supports a guarded Haxe runtime bootstrap via @:wp.haxeBootstrap, and fixtures/wphx-php/f1-facade.hxml emits build/wphx-php/f1/generated/wp-includes/plugin.php for add_filter. npm run wphx:php:f1 passes PHP lint plus the existing F1 reflection/behavior oracle probe and verifies manifest declaration global-function:add_filter. WPHX-COMP-PHP.01 remains open because F4 public-class compiler driver is still pending.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-COMP-PHP.01: F1 and F4 facade fixtures now have wphx-php compiler variants. npm run wphx:php:f1:check emits wp-includes/plugin.php for add_filter, passes PHP lint and the existing F1 reflection/behavior oracle probe, and records global-function:add_filter. npm run wphx:php:f4:check emits wp-includes/class-wphx-public-class.php for the public interface/base/class ABI, passes PHP lint and the existing F4 reflection/object-behavior oracle probe, preserves inheritance/constants/static/protected/public properties/method reflection, and records interface:WPHX_Public_Interface, class:WPHX_Public_Base, class:WPHX_Public_Class. The emitter also records the stdlib reuse rule so WordPress-specific lowering remains bounded.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP — Build in-repo Reflaxe PHP emitter
description: Build an in-repo Haxe/Reflaxe PHP emitter that can generate WordPress-shaped original-path PHP files from Haxe source and metadata. This supersedes the repeated manually generated PHP shell candidate treadmill and should be driven by existing PHP facade/linker fixtures plus one small WordPress public-method driver before broader Core adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler source lives in this repo; Reflaxe output is explicitly pinned; wphx-php smoke/check scripts compile and lint generated PHP; an emission manifest records source modules, WordPress output paths, declarations, hashes, and unsupported constructs; at least one facade fixture and one small WPHX-312 public-method shell are driven by compiler-emitted PHP or the remaining blocker is captured as compiler-pressure evidence.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-COMP-PHP: the in-repo Reflaxe PHP emitter lives under src/wphx/compiler/php, uses the pinned Reflaxe classpath, has smoke/F1/F4/WP_Http chunkTransferDecode check scripts, writes wphx-php-emission.v1.json manifests, drives F1 add_filter and F4 public-class facade fixtures through compiler-emitted PHP, and drives the WPHX-312.61 WP_Http::chunkTransferDecode original-path public method through compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php. Future work should expand grouped original-path/linker emission and broader WP_Http helpers before the full request method.

title: Ask oracle to review WordPress original-path PHP emission strategy
description: Capture an oracle review prompt for the filesystem/API emission decision: whether wordpresshx should continue with stock Haxe PHP output plus deterministic original-path linker/shell generation, augment/fork the Haxe PHP generator, or pursue a custom/Reflaxe PHP target. The review should evaluate WordPress compatibility requirements for original file paths, require/include behavior, public function/class/trait/interface ABI, conditional declarations, globals/references, reflection, stack traces, source maps, generated target readability, plugin/theme ecosystem expectations, compiler-pressure criteria, and migration sequencing. This is architecture review only and should not block narrow Haxe parity candidates already using temporary shells.
notes:
Oracle prompt context: We are porting WordPress 7.0 runtime logic to Haxe while preserving the public WordPress PHP filesystem/API interface. Current candidates compile Haxe to generated PHP library paths and use temporary original-path PHP shells in copied oracle files. The architecture question is whether durable distribution emission should be: (1) stock Haxe PHP target plus deterministic original-path linker/shell emitter driven by Haxe metadata/macros; (2) generic augmentations/fork of the Haxe PHP generator so selected Haxe modules can emit WordPress-shaped files directly; or (3) custom/Reflaxe PHP target. Please evaluate compatibility risk for require/include path identity, conditional declarations, global functions, classes, traits, interfaces, references, globals, reflection-visible signatures, stack traces/source maps, plugin/theme expectations, generated target readability, compiler-pressure criteria, migration sequencing, and when temporary shells become unacceptable. Recommend evidence gates and ADR criteria.

User strategy note: Reflaxe is an acceptable candidate for the custom generator path if evidence shows we need one. If pursued, follow the pattern of prior sibling compiler work in ../haxe.rust, ../haxe.go, ../haxe.elixir.codex, and relevant examples under ../haxe.compilerdev.reference. Current direction remains stock Haxe PHP plus deterministic original-path linker/shell emitter first, with compiler-pressure receipts before escalating to augmented/forked/custom generation.
close_reason: Captured the reproducible oracle prompt bundle for original-path PHP emission strategy in docs/operations/oracle-original-path-php-emission-review.md, including current WPHX PHP emitter evidence, strategy options, escalation criteria questions, and ADR follow-up expectations. Added an operations receipt and linked the prompt from docs/operations/oracle.md.

title: WPHX-309.08 - Refresh packaged ABI evidence after routing link fixture
description: Refresh WPHX-700 packaged ABI no-fallback evidence after WPHX-309.06/WPHX-309.07 changed upstream evidence provenance. This is a bounded receipt/manifest freshness update and does not expand wpdb parity claims.
close_reason: Refreshed WPHX-700 packaged ABI no-fallback evidence after WPHX-309.06 routing/link fixture package provenance changed; local regenerate and check passed.

title: WPHX-308.05 — Add taxonomy/comment query-state oracle fixture
description: Add a deterministic oracle-source-mirror fixture for WordPress 7.0 taxonomy/comment query-state behavior after WPHX-308.04. It should cover selected WP_Term_Query and WP_Comment_Query query var normalization, SQL clause construction, cache-key/signature behavior where observable, and hook/filter observations without claiming generated public PHP replacement or live database parity.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic runner, manifest, ownership manifest, receipt, package script, CI deterministic check, progress doc update, and Beads notes cover focused taxonomy/comment query-state behavior. The evidence must remain scoped as oracle-source-mirror and must not claim Haxe-owned public PHP replacement, installed behavior, or live DB parity.
notes: Follows WPHX-308.04. Keep this focused on query-state and SQL/clause behavior; live DB taxonomy/comment parity and upstream PHPUnit ratchets remain later gates.

title: WPHX-308.04 — Add taxonomy/comment count and cache invalidation fixture

notes: Run 28052494069 failed in wp-core-live-db-parity at tools/wp-core/run-rest-server-db-browser-matrix.mjs page.evaluate with TypeError: Failed to fetch. close_reason: Hardened the WPHX-311.10 REST DB browser harness by suppressing automatic favicon traffic, waiting for network idle before browser fetch cases, refreshing manifests/receipts, and passing the focused local DB-browser gate.
