title: WPHX-305.23 — Implement typed wpdb public-state descriptor shell proof
description: Build on WPHX-305.22 by implementing the selected minimal wpdb public-state ownership slice: a typed Haxe public-state descriptor plus a PHP-visible shell/reflection/drop-in proof that preserves the WordPress/plugin wpdb ABI before any bootstrap or property storage migration.
acceptance_criteria: A typed Haxe WpdbPublicStateDescriptor or equivalent compiles without Dynamic, untyped, broad casts, raw php.Syntax.code, or generated PHP postprocessing; a reflection/drop-in shell probe compares declared public properties, magic-visible internals, protected write-block names, #[AllowDynamicProperties], and require_wp_db()/db.php replacement timing against the WordPress 7.0 oracle; WPHX-305.21 and WPHX-305.20 live gates remain green; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining WPHX-305 gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.23 typed wpdb public-state descriptor shell proof. Added WpdbPublicStateDescriptor with typed Haxe inventories and policy helpers for declared public properties, magic-visible internal properties, public magic accessors, protected __set write-block properties, dynamic property preservation, db.php replacement preservation, and field_types direct mutation. Added a candidate runner that compiles the descriptor to PHP, compares generated descriptor output against WordPress 7.0 ReflectionClass("wpdb"), probes constructor-free wpdb::__set behavior for protected write-block names, and verifies require_wp_db() preserves a wp-content/db.php supplied global $wpdb without invoking the default constructor. Verified WPHX-305.23 generate/check, WPHX-305.21 row materialization live gate, WPHX-305.20 mysqli @:phpGlobal live gate, Haxe formatting for new files, escape-hatch audit, baseline validation, and Beads validation. Filed WPHX-305.24 for the public-state storage adapter proof.

title: WPHX-305.22 — Prove wpdb drop-in/public state ownership boundary
description: Build on WPHX-305.21 by planning and proving the next bounded wpdb ownership slice after typed row materialization: how the port can preserve the plugin/drop-in-visible PHP wpdb class ABI while moving public mutable state ownership, load timing, and replacement/drop-in behavior toward typed Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: A follow-on candidate or design receipt identifies the minimal wpdb drop-in/public-state ownership slice, preserves the WordPress/plugin-facing wpdb class interface and mutable public fields, keeps WPHX-305.21 row materialization and WPHX-305.20 mysqli @:phpGlobal execution green on MySQL/MariaDB oracle gates, and updates manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining WPHX-305 gaps without new Dynamic, untyped, broad casts, raw php.Syntax.code, or generated PHP postprocessing.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-305.22 wpdb drop-in/public-state ownership boundary plan. Added a structured php-parser based generator/check for the upstream WordPress 7.0 wpdb public property, magic accessor, dynamic property, require_wp_db(), db.php replacement, and wp_set_wpdb_vars() contracts. Recorded the selected next slice as a typed WpdbPublicStateDescriptor plus PHP shell/reflection/drop-in proof, preserving the PHP-visible wpdb ABI before moving public mutable state storage. Verified WPHX-305.22 generate/check, WPHX-305.21 row materialization live gate, WPHX-305.20 mysqli @:phpGlobal live gate, Haxe formatting, escape-hatch audit, baseline validation, Beads validation, and receipts validation. Filed WPHX-305.23 for the typed descriptor shell proof.

title: WPHX-305.21 — Type wpdb fetched row object materialization
description: Build on WPHX-305.20 by modeling mysqli_fetch_object() row materialization and WordPress stdClass result consumption more precisely in typed Haxe while preserving wpdb public result-shape ABI. The 305.20 candidate already routes mysqli_fetch_object() through @:phpGlobal generated Haxe PHP; this task should reduce the remaining PHP-shell row-object handling smell without breaking get_var/get_row/get_results behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A live wpdb candidate keeps direct @:phpGlobal mysqli_fetch_object() execution and models fetched row presence, assignment, and field/value materialization with typed Haxe-owned helpers; get_var/get_row/get_col/get_results OBJECT, OBJECT_K, ARRAY_A, and ARRAY_N oracle comparisons still pass on MySQL and MariaDB; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining WPHX-305 gaps are updated without Dynamic, untyped, broad casts, raw php.Syntax.code, or generated PHP postprocessing.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.21 typed wpdb fetched-row materialization. Added WpdbRowMaterialization and RowGlobal typed @:phpGlobal row/stdClass helpers, plus a live row-materialization candidate that keeps WpdbMysqliExecution direct mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() lowering while moving fetched-row object presence, get_var value extraction, get_row ARRAY_A/ARRAY_N materialization, get_results ARRAY_A/ARRAY_N materialization, and OBJECT_K first-field key extraction out of the generated wpdb shell and into generated Haxe PHP helpers. Verified MySQL 8.4 and MariaDB 11.8 oracle comparisons with 18 fixture cases and 2 matching comparisons; WPHX-305.21 generate/check, WPHX-305.20 predecessor check, WPHX-305.19 lowering proof check, Haxe formatting, escape-hatch audit with no new occurrences, baseline validation, Beads validation, and receipts validation. Filed WPHX-305.22 for wpdb drop-in/public-state ownership.

title: WPHX-305.20 — Integrate @:phpGlobal mysqli externs into live wpdb candidate
description: Build on WPHX-305.19 by replacing the generated WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary query/fetch calls in a live wpdb candidate with the proven typed @:phpGlobal MysqliGlobal.query() and MysqliGlobal.fetchObject() extern path. Preserve WordPress-compatible wpdb PHP method shape and public mutable state while removing the two-method generated PHP mysqli boundary.
acceptance_criteria: A live wpdb candidate emits direct mysqli_query($handle, $query) and mysqli_fetch_object($result) global PHP calls through typed Haxe @:phpGlobal externs; WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary is removed or superseded in the candidate shell; MySQL and MariaDB live gates compare against the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining WPHX-305 gaps are updated without Dynamic, untyped, broad casts, raw php.Syntax.code, or generated PHP postprocessing.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.20 live wpdb @:phpGlobal mysqli integration. Added typed WpdbMysqliCallGate and WpdbMysqliExecution helpers, plus a WpdbMysqliPhpGlobalCandidate fixture and live DB runner. The generated Haxe PHP emits direct \mysqli_query($handle, $query) and \mysqli_fetch_object($result) through the typed MysqliGlobal @:phpGlobal externs; the WPHX_305_17 generated mysqli boundary is removed from the WPHX-305.20 candidate shell and replaced by \wphx\wp\db\WpdbMysqliExecution::query()/fetchObject() calls. Verified WPHX-305.20 against WordPress 7.0 oracle behavior on locked MySQL 8.4 and MariaDB 11.8 runtimes with 18 fixture cases and 2 matching comparisons, plus WPHX-305.19 lowering proof check, WPHX-305.17 row traversal check, Haxe formatting, escape-hatch audit, Beads validation, and receipts validation. Filed WPHX-305.21 for typed fetched-row object materialization.

title: WPHX-305.19 — Scaffold wpdb PHP target global-function lowering proof
description: Build on WPHX-305.18 by scaffolding the selected PHP target/Reflaxe or stock-Haxe-backend proof for typed receiver-less PHP global function lowering. The proof should emit idiomatic mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() calls from typed Haxe-owned call sites, preserving WordPress/plugin-visible PHP shape and avoiding raw php.Syntax.code, Dynamic, untyped, broad casts, or generated PHP postprocessing.
acceptance_criteria: A runnable compiler/backend proof emits direct mysqli_query( $dbh, $query ) and mysqli_fetch_object( $result ) global PHP calls from typed Haxe call specs; generated PHP is lint-clean and idiomatic enough for WordPress/plugin reflection; WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary removal or continued isolation is decided from evidence; WPHX-305 manifests, ownership, receipts, and live database gates are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.19 stock Haxe PHP global-function lowering proof. Added typed MysqliHandle/MysqliResult externs and @:phpGlobal MysqliGlobal extern, plus a kept proof fixture showing generated PHP emits direct \mysqli_query($handle, $query) and \mysqli_fetch_object($result) without class-static calls, Dynamic, untyped, casts, php.Syntax.code, or PHP postprocessing. Recorded WPHX-305.18 correction that custom/Reflaxe is not needed for this specific mysqli global-call gap, kept WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary until live candidate integration, and filed WPHX-305.20 for boundary replacement in the live wpdb candidate. Verified new proof/check, WPHX-305.18 check, WPHX-305.17 live row traversal, Haxe formatting, escape-hatch audit, Beads validation, and receipts validation.

title: WPHX-305.18 — Decide wpdb mysqli global-call lowering path
description: Build on WPHX-305.17 by deciding and proving the path for direct idiomatic PHP global mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() emission from typed Haxe. Candidate paths include improving the stock Haxe PHP target, adding a narrow reflaxe/custom PHP target lowering rule, or documenting why this must wait for a broader PHP target fork. The current WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary remains the temporary target-native isolation point.
acceptance_criteria: A compiler or target strategy is selected with a runnable proof or explicit deferral evidence; generated PHP for the wpdb query/fetch path remains WordPress/plugin compatible and reflection-safe; any direct lowering emits idiomatic mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() global calls without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining WPHX-305 gaps are updated.
close_reason: Selected the WPHX-305.18 wpdb mysqli global-call lowering path. Added a runnable stock Haxe PHP probe showing static externs lower to class-static MysqliFunctions::mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() calls, and leading-backslash native names still produce invalid class-static PHP rather than receiver-less globals. Recorded reference evidence from Reflaxe and sibling custom targets, selected a Reflaxe/custom PHP global-function intrinsic path, kept WPHX_305_17_MysqliBoundary as the temporary ABI-safe boundary, and filed WPHX-305.19 for the concrete backend proof. Verified the new generator/check, inherited WPHX-305.17 live candidate, Haxe formatting, escape-hatch audit, Beads validation, and receipts validation.

title: WPHX-305.17 — Promote wpdb mysqli direct-lowering and row traversal ownership slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.16 by replacing or further narrowing the generated WPHX_305_16_MysqliBoundary around mysqli_query() and mysqli_fetch_object(). Candidate paths include a stock Haxe PHP target improvement, a reflaxe/custom PHP lowering proof, typed mysqli_result traversal ownership, native row-object assignment ownership, or evidence that a dedicated compiler target is required before the raw PHP boundary can be removed.
acceptance_criteria: mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() are emitted through idiomatic PHP-compatible code from a typed Haxe-owned path or remain isolated behind an even narrower explicitly justified boundary; wpdb public ABI and plugin/drop-in compatibility remain unchanged; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare against the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.17 mysqli row traversal candidate. Added typed Haxe WpdbRowTraversal decisions for fetched-row presence, last_result assignment slot, and row-count advancement; generated the live MySQL/MariaDB row traversal candidate on top of WPHX-305.16; kept mysqli_query()/mysqli_fetch_object() isolated in the generated PHP boundary with explicit compiler-pressure evidence for future PHP target/reflaxe work. Verified WPHX-305.17 generate/check, inherited WPHX-305.16 through live DB gates, Haxe format, escape-hatch audit, Beads validation, and receipts validation. Filed WPHX-305.18 for direct global-call lowering strategy.

title: WPHX-305.16 — Promote wpdb mysqli query/result/fetch ownership candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.15 by attacking the remaining raw mysqli query/result/fetch boundary. Candidate paths include typed target-native mysqli externs, a narrow generated PHP/native resource facade, mysqli_query isolation, mysqli_result traversal ownership, mysqli_fetch_object row materialization policy, last_result object assignment ownership, or compiler-pressure evidence that stock Haxe PHP target cannot emit idiomatic enough PHP without a custom target/reflaxe path.
acceptance_criteria: A selected mysqli_query/mysqli_result/mysqli_fetch_object ownership slice is implemented in typed Haxe or an explicitly justified narrow target-native boundary without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; public wpdb PHP class shape remains WordPress-compatible for plugins/reflection/drop-ins; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare to the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.16 mysqli query/result/fetch boundary candidate. Added typed Haxe WpdbMysqliBoundary decisions, generated/live-checked wpdb candidate shell, compiler-pressure evidence for stock Haxe PHP global mysqli extern lowering, manifests/ownership/receipt, and npm check scripts. Verified with WPHX-305.16 live MySQL/MariaDB candidate/check plus inherited WPHX-305 gates, haxe escape-hatch audit, Haxe format, Beads validation, and receipts validation. Filed WPHX-305.17 for direct typed lowering/row traversal follow-up.

title: WPHX-305.15 — Promote wpdb raw mysqli resource boundary candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.14 by narrowing the remaining raw mysqli/resource boundary after selected-row population policy is Haxe-owned. Candidate areas include typed target-native mysqli_result/resource externs, raw mysqli_query isolation, mysqli_fetch_object ownership strategy, last_result object assignment seam, reconnect retry completion state, replacement/drop-in compatibility, or compiler-pressure evidence if the stock PHP target cannot emit idiomatic enough PHP for this boundary.
acceptance_criteria: A selected raw mysqli/resource boundary slice is implemented in typed Haxe or a narrower explicitly documented target-native boundary without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; public wpdb PHP class shape remains WordPress-compatible for plugins/reflection/drop-ins; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare to the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.15 raw mysqli resource metadata candidate: added typed WpdbRawResource guards/fallbacks, live MySQL/MariaDB oracle comparison with metadata and empty-handle reconnect coverage, manifests/ownership/receipt, npm scripts, and WPHX-305.16 follow-up for actual mysqli query/result/fetch ownership.

title: WPHX-305.14 — Promote wpdb result population and native mysqli boundary candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.13 by narrowing the remaining wpdb native mysqli/result boundary after _do_query policy is Haxe-owned. Candidate areas include raw mysqli_query isolation, mysqli_result/resource typing, last_result population loop ownership, reconnect retry completion state, replacement/drop-in seams, and distribution strategy if the stock PHP target cannot emit idiomatic enough PHP.
acceptance_criteria: A selected wpdb result/native boundary slice is implemented in typed Haxe or a narrower explicitly documented target-native boundary without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; public wpdb class shape remains WordPress-compatible for plugins/reflection/drop-ins; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare to the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.14 result-population candidate: typed Haxe owns selected-row population guard, row-count initialization/increment, and selected-row return value while preserving native mysqli_fetch_object row objects and WordPress-compatible wpdb last_result/num_rows public state. Live MySQL/MariaDB oracle gates and inherited WPHX-305 checks pass. Filed WPHX-305.15 for the remaining raw mysqli/resource boundary.

title: WPHX-305.13 — Promote wpdb native execution boundary candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.12 by narrowing the remaining native wpdb query execution boundary while preserving WordPress-compatible public PHP class shape, mysqli behavior, replacement/drop-in seams, and plugin-facing state. Candidate areas include _do_query call ownership, reconnect/error transition modeling, last_result population loop ownership, query logging timing, or a typed target-native PHP boundary if direct mysqli ownership remains target constrained.
acceptance_criteria: A selected wpdb native execution-boundary slice is implemented in typed Haxe or explicitly isolated behind a narrower typed PHP boundary without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; public wpdb methods/properties and replacement/drop-in behavior remain compatible; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare to the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.13 native-execution candidate: typed Haxe owns _do_query SAVEQUERIES logging policy, native execution guard, num_queries increment, and reconnect retry decision while preserving WordPress wpdb PHP shape; live MySQL/MariaDB oracle gates and inherited checks pass. Filed WPHX-305.14 for remaining mysqli/result population boundary.

title: WPHX-305.12 — Promote wpdb query execution/state Haxe candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.11 by moving the next bounded wpdb class behavior slice behind typed Haxe while preserving WordPress-compatible public PHP class shape, mutable state, and plugin-facing behavior. Candidate areas include query outcome classification, last_result/num_rows/rows_affected/insert_id state decisions, and error-state transitions around live DB execution. This should keep native mysqli execution behind the current boundary unless a narrower typed PHP target boundary is proven.
acceptance_criteria: A selected wpdb query execution/state slice is implemented in typed Haxe without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or php.Syntax.code; public wpdb methods/properties and replacement/drop-in seams remain WordPress-compatible; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare to the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Promoted bounded wpdb::query classification/return-state decisions into typed Haxe while preserving native mysqli execution and public PHP wpdb ABI; live MySQL/MariaDB oracle comparisons pass and WPHX-305.13 tracks the remaining native execution boundary.

title: WPHX-305.11 — Promote wpdb query/result Haxe candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.10 by moving a bounded wpdb class query/result behavior slice behind typed Haxe while preserving WordPress-compatible public PHP class shape, mutable state, result object/array contracts, and live database execution. Candidate areas include query outcome/state classification, get_var/get_row/get_col/get_results selection behavior, or affected-row/insert-id state decisions selected by risk and fixture coverage.
acceptance_criteria: A selected wpdb query/result behavior slice is implemented in typed Haxe without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, or php.Syntax.code escape hatches; public wpdb methods/properties and plugin-facing PHP behavior remain WordPress-compatible; live MySQL/MariaDB candidate gates compare against the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Promoted bounded wpdb get_var/get_row/get_results result-selection decisions into typed Haxe with live MySQL/MariaDB oracle comparison and follow-up WPHX-305.12 filed for query execution/state ownership.

title: WPHX-305.10 — Promote next wpdb/dbDelta Haxe candidate slice
description: Build on WPHX-305.08 by moving the next meaningful wpdb/database abstraction slice behind typed Haxe while preserving WordPress-compatible PHP public surfaces. Candidate areas include dbDelta schema parsing/planning or a bounded wpdb query/result decision path, selected by risk and live-DB fixture coverage.
acceptance_criteria: A selected wpdb or dbDelta behavior slice is implemented in typed Haxe without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, or php.Syntax.code escape hatches; public PHP function/class signatures and plugin-facing behavior remain WordPress-compatible; the WPHX-305 live DB candidate gate compares against the WordPress 7.0 oracle on locked MySQL and MariaDB; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented the WPHX-305.10 dbDelta Haxe candidate slice. The new DbDeltaPlanner typed Haxe helper promotes dbDelta query classification, target object extraction, field/index-line classification, and index type/name normalization while preserving WordPress-compatible PHP global function shells and real wpdb SQL execution. The WPHX-305.10 generator runs the inherited live MySQL/MariaDB WPHX-305.07/WPHX-305.08 cases against the Haxe-transformed candidate root, emits manifest/ownership/receipt artifacts, and records WPHX-305.11 for the remaining broader wpdb query/result ownership gap. Verified npm run wp:core:wphx-305-dbdelta-candidate, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-dbdelta-candidate:check, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db-candidate:check, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db:check, npm run beads:validate, and npm run receipts:validate.

title: WPHX-305.08 — Add typed Haxe wpdb/schema/option live-DB candidate
description: Move the first selected wpdb, schema helper, and database-backed option/transient paths from oracle-source mirrors into typed Haxe candidates, using WPHX-305.07 as the live MySQL/MariaDB runtime gate. This is not a typed facade over WordPress: use the oracle fixtures to preserve public PHP plugin compatibility while letting typed Haxe own behavior and improve internal smells where parity permits.
acceptance_criteria: Typed Haxe candidates cover an agreed first slice of wpdb/schema/option behavior without unjustified Dynamic or broad php.Syntax.code escape hatches; generated PHP keeps the WordPress-compatible public interface; WPHX-305.07 live DB gate compares candidate behavior against the WordPress 7.0 oracle; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Implemented the first typed Haxe live-DB candidate for WPHX-305. The new LiveDbSchema Haxe helper promotes maybe_create_table() and maybe_add_column() branch decisions while preserving WordPress-compatible PHP global function shells and real wpdb SQL execution. The WPHX-305.08 generator runs the inherited live MySQL/MariaDB WPHX-305.07 cases against the Haxe-transformed candidate root, emits manifest/ownership/receipt artifacts, and records follow-ups WPHX-305.09 and WPHX-305.10 for remaining PHP DB-client and broader wpdb/dbDelta ownership gaps. Verified npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db-candidate, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db-candidate:check, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db:check, npm run haxe:escape-hatches:check, npm run beads:validate, and npm run receipts:validate.

title: WPHX-305.07 — Add live MySQL/MariaDB database runtime gate
description: Use the WPHX-305 deterministic schema/option integration fixture as the seed set for a live database runtime gate. Cover real MySQL/MariaDB DDL/DML execution for dbDelta/maybe_create_table/maybe_add_column, option/transient storage, information-schema/DESCRIBE/SHOW INDEX metadata, SQL modes, permissions/error paths, and locking/transaction boundaries that the deterministic wpdb subclass intentionally records as native execution gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Harness provisions locked MySQL/MariaDB runtimes, runs the WPHX-305.06 seed cases against live databases, compares stable observable behavior to the WordPress 7.0 oracle, emits manifests/ownership/receipts, and updates the remaining native database execution boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented the WPHX-305.07 live MySQL/MariaDB runtime gate. The generator provisions locked mysql-8.4 and mariadb-11.8 containers, runs the WPHX-305.06 schema/option seed cases plus live SQL mode, metadata, permission-error, transaction, and locking checks, compares oracle-source mirror behavior for WordPress 7.0, and emits manifest, ownership, and receipt artifacts. Verified npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db, npm run wp:core:wphx-305-live-db:check, npm run beads:validate, npm run receipts:validate, and npm run precommit before closure. Follow-ups WPHX-305.08 and WPHX-305.09 cover the typed Haxe candidate and PHP DB-client matrix gaps.

title: WPHX-305.06 — Build dbDelta and option-storage database integration harness
description: Use WPHX-305 database fixtures plus WPHX-304 option/cache fixtures to cover dbDelta/maybe_create_table/maybe_add_column and the real database-backed option/transient storage paths that were previously deterministic wpdb doubles.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares schema-upgrade and option/transient database-backed storage seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle; receipts link WPHX-304 database gaps to WPHX-305 ownership gates and identify any remaining native database execution boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-305.06 schema/option/transient integration fixture harness with manifests, ownership, receipts, package scripts, deterministic checks, and WPHX-305.07 follow-up for live MySQL/MariaDB execution.

title: WPHX-305.05 — Build charset/collation and prefix fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-305.01 surface manifest to build oracle fixtures for charset/collation setup, table charset discovery, safe collation checks, table lists, prefix/blog switching, parse_db_host(), and bootstrap vars.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares charset/collation/prefix seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifests and receipts record MySQL server/native boundaries, multisite table prefix behavior, bootstrap constants, and Haxe ownership gates.
close_reason: Added wpdb charset/collation and prefix oracle fixture harness covering single-site and multisite bootstrap constants, wp_set_wpdb_vars(), prefix/blog switching, table scope matrices, parse_db_host(), server capability gates, table charset metadata interpretation, column charset/length lookup, and safe-collation checks across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Evidence: manifests/wp-core/wphx-305-05-wpdb-charset-prefix-fixture.v1.json and receipts/wp-core/wphx-305-05-wpdb-charset-prefix-fixture.v1.json.

title: WPHX-305.04 — Build write/process-field fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-305.01 surface manifest to build oracle fixtures for insert(), replace(), update(), delete(), process_fields(), field formats, null handling, charset/length guards, and affected-row/insert-id state.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares write/process-field seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt record field-format coercion, null SQL shape, charset/length native checks, errors, and state mutation boundaries.
close_reason: Added wpdb write/process-field oracle fixture harness covering insert/replace/update/delete SQL shape, field format coercion, null handling, charset/length guard errors, strip_invalid_text truncation, and deterministic write-state mutation across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Evidence: manifests/wp-core/wphx-305-04-wpdb-write-fields-fixture.v1.json and receipts/wp-core/wphx-305-04-wpdb-write-fields-fixture.v1.json.

title: WPHX-305.03 — Build query and read-result fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-305.01 surface manifest to build oracle fixtures for query(), get_var(), get_row(), get_col(), get_results(), result shapes, last_query/last_result/num_rows/rows_affected, and error reporting state.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares query/read seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; result object/array shapes, public mutable state, error branches, and mysqli/native boundaries are recorded.
close_reason: Built the WPHX-305.03 wpdb query/read-result differential fixture harness. It mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 source into oracle and candidate roots, covers 9 query/result symbols with 10 seed traces, compares local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5 runtimes, and records deterministic query/result and column-metadata test-double boundaries plus the remaining live mysqli_result traversal, charset/collation, and full upstream PHPUnit gaps.

title: WPHX-305.02 — Build prepare and escaping fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-305.01 surface manifest to build oracle fixtures for wpdb::prepare(), placeholders, identifier quoting, esc_like(), esc_sql(), placeholder escape tokens, and by-reference escaping behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares prepare/escaping seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify placeholder, quoting, mysqli escape, filter/native, and by-reference boundaries.
close_reason: Built the WPHX-305.02 wpdb prepare/escaping differential fixture harness. It mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 source into oracle and candidate roots, covers 13 prepare/escaping symbols with 14 seed traces, normalizes wpdb placeholder tokens, compares local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5 runtimes, and records mysqli real-escape, placeholder query-filter, doing_it_wrong diagnostics, and PHP by-reference mutation boundaries plus remaining live-MySQL/query-execution gaps.

title: WPHX-305.01 — Inventory wpdb/database surface
description: Build the first WPHX-305 database surface manifest from the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle and existing ABI/test inventories. The manifest should enumerate wpdb source units, bootstrap/boundary functions, public class properties/methods/constants, upstream database tests, risk categories, and first differential fixture targets before database logic moves into Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic WPHX-305.01 tool generates a manifest, ownership record, and receipt for wpdb/database boundaries; dotted refs validate; manifest identifies source units, ABI symbols, upstream tests, fixture seeds, native mysqli/db.php boundaries, and follow-up port slices; checks and receipt validation pass.
close_reason: Generated deterministic WPHX-305.01 wpdb/database surface inventory, ownership manifest, and receipt. The manifest covers 9 upstream source units, 9 database domains, 171 ABI symbol selections, 20 upstream database/option test links, native mysqli/db-dropin/dead-db boundaries, deferred query-domain consumers, and follow-up fixture slices WPHX-305.02 through WPHX-305.06. Verified npm run wp:core:wphx-305-surface:check and npm run beads:validate.

title: WPHX-305 — wpdb and database abstraction
description: Port and validate WordPress wpdb and database abstraction behavior for the 7.0 line. Start from the WPHX-304 option/transient fixtures that currently use deterministic wpdb test doubles, then build a database surface inventory and differential fixtures for query preparation, reads/writes, result objects, by-reference behaviors, errors, prefixes, charset/collation, and storage paths used by options/cache domains.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic WPHX-305 surface manifest and oracle fixture suite compare the locked WordPress 7.0 wpdb/database behavior across local PHP and pinned runtimes; fixtures cover prepare/query/get_var/get_row/get_results/insert/update/delete, result object shape, errors, prefixes, charset/collation, and option/cache storage paths currently doubled in WPHX-304; ownership manifests and receipts identify PHP-native database boundaries and Haxe ownership gates.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-305 wpdb/database abstraction workset: surface inventory, deterministic fixtures, live MySQL/MariaDB gates, typed Haxe candidate slices through prepare/query/db_connect/public-state/native mysqli boundaries, locked PHP DB-client runtime matrix, manifests, ownership records, and receipts are in place.

title: WPHX-304.07 — Promote first pure options/cache helpers to Haxe parity candidates
description: After the option, object-cache, and serialization fixtures are stable, identify the first low-risk pure helper branches from the WPHX-304 surface and promote them to typed Haxe parity candidates without broad Dynamic, raw PHP string emission, or plugin-incompatible ABI changes.
acceptance_criteria: Selected option/cache helper candidates have typed Haxe implementations, generated PHP stays idiomatic for WordPress plugin compatibility, any unavoidable PHP-native boundary is isolated and justified, and oracle/candidate receipts pass across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations.
close_reason: Closed

title: WPHX-304.06 — Build settings/default-option registry fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-304.01 surface manifest to build an oracle fixture harness for settings registration, settings unregistration, registered-setting shape, default option filters, sanitize callbacks, and REST schema boundary notes.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares settings/default-option registry seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify global registry shape, filter/default behavior, callback/native boundaries, REST schema dependencies, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Closed

title: WPHX-304.05 — Build serialization, cache salt, and invalidation fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-304.01 surface manifest to build an oracle fixture harness for PHP serialization helpers, is_serialized predicates, last_changed cache salts, salted cache helpers, and cache suspension/invalidation flags.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares serialization, last_changed, salted cache, and cache suspension seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify native PHP serialization boundaries, hook emissions, cache group salt behavior, global flag state, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Added serialization/cache salt/invalidation fixture harness, manifest, ownership record, receipt, and validation scripts.

title: WPHX-304.04 — Build object cache runtime/drop-in fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-304.01 surface manifest to build an oracle fixture harness for the default object-cache runtime, public wp_cache_* APIs, groups, found-reference behavior, increments/decrements, flushes, blog switching, and persistent drop-in compatibility shims.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares object-cache runtime and drop-in compatibility seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify covered symbols, WP_Object_Cache public shape, global state, group semantics, reference outputs, drop-in boundaries, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Added object cache runtime/drop-in fixture harness, manifest, ownership record, receipt, and validation scripts.

title: WPHX-304.03 — Build transient and site-transient expiration fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-304.01 surface manifest to build an oracle fixture harness for regular transients, site transients, timeout/value pairing, expiration, deletion, no-timeout updates, and the feed-cache transient bridge.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares transient and site-transient seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify covered symbols, timeout/value storage, expiration cleanup, feed-cache bridge behavior, native wpdb/cache/filter boundaries, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Built the WPHX-304.03 transient/site-transient/feed-cache differential harness. It mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 source into oracle and candidate roots, covers 15 transient/feed-cache symbols with 12 seed traces, compares local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5 runtimes, and records native option-table wpdb test-double, single-site site-transient, object-cache, plugin hook, SimplePie stub, and PHP time boundaries plus WPHX-305, WPHX-304.04, and multisite gaps.

title: WPHX-304.02 — Build option storage/autoload differential fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-304.01 surface manifest to build an oracle fixture harness for option storage, alloptions/notoptions cache behavior, autoload transitions, false/default contracts, serialization, and option filter hooks before Haxe implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares get/add/update/delete option and alloptions/notoptions/autoload seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify covered symbols, source units, native wpdb/cache/filter boundaries, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Built the WPHX-304.02 option storage/autoload differential harness. It mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 source into oracle and candidate roots, covers 15 option/autoload symbols with 12 seed traces, compares local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5 runtimes, and records native wpdb test-double, object-cache, plugin hook, KSES escaping, and PHP serialization boundaries plus remaining WPHX-305 and Haxe-candidate gaps.

title: WPHX-304.01 — Inventory options/transients/object-cache surface
description: Build the first WPHX-304 domain surface manifest from the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle and existing ABI/test inventories. The manifest should enumerate option, transient, object-cache, drop-in compatibility, source units, public symbols, upstream tests, risk categories, and first differential fixture targets before storage/cache logic moves into Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic WPHX-304.01 tool generates a manifest and receipt for options, transients, object cache, cache drop-in compatibility, and storage/cache boundaries; dotted refs validate; manifest identifies source units, ABI symbols, upstream tests, fixture seeds, ownership state, and follow-up port slices; checks and receipt validation pass.
close_reason: Generated the WPHX-304.01 options/transients/object-cache surface manifest, external-oracle ownership record, and receipt from WordPress 7.0 source/test/ABI inventories; identified 128 public symbols, 31 upstream tests, seven domain partitions, three deferred cross-domain behaviors, and filed WPHX-304.02 through WPHX-304.07 follow-up slices.

title: WPHX-304 — Options, transients, object cache
description: Port and validate WordPress options, transients, and object cache behavior in behavior-first source-unit slices. Start with deterministic surface inventory and differential storage/cache invalidation traces against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle before any Haxe ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Option, transient, and object-cache fixtures compare the WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; traces cover add/update/delete/get options, alloptions/notoptions cache behavior, autoload decisions, site and network option paths where feasible, transient timeout/value pairing and expiration, wp_cache add/set/get/delete/incr/decr/flush/groups, invalidation hooks, and ownership manifests/receipts record Haxe-owned versus PHP-native storage/cache boundaries.
close_reason: Closed after WPHX-304.01 through WPHX-304.07 completed. Fresh audit passed surface, option-storage, transient, object-cache, serialization/cache, settings/defaults, option-cache candidate, Haxe escape-hatch, Beads graph, and receipt validation checks. Remaining non-closure gaps are now tracked by WPHX-305 (wpdb/database abstraction), WPHX-311 (REST settings/schema), WPHX-317 (multisite/network site-option and site-transient paths), and WPHX-322 (PHP first-party manifest/upstream PHPUnit closure).

title: WPHX-700.01 — Expand KSES security corpus and block/feed integration gates
description: Follow-up from WPHX-303.05. Add a broader KSES parity/security gate that imports more upstream KSES PHPUnit coverage or a generated XSS corpus, covers block attribute sanitization through parse_blocks()/filter_block_kses(), and exercises the full SimplePie feed sanitizer stack including encoding/registry behavior before any KSES Haxe ownership promotion.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic gate compares WordPress 7.0 oracle and candidate behavior across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; block attribute sanitization, full SimplePie bridge behavior without parent stubs, and broad protocol/entity/attribute/CSS XSS cases are covered; receipts and ownership manifests record remaining native boundaries and promotion/removal gates.
close_reason: Added WPHX-700.01 KSES security corpus and block/feed integration gate. The gate mirrors WordPress 7.0 oracle/candidate trees, loads the real SimplePie tree and registry, covers generated XSS corpus cases, protocol/entity/attribute/CSS boundaries, parse_blocks/filter_block_content/filter_block_kses block attribute sanitization, and registry-backed WP_SimplePie_Sanitize_KSES behavior across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Verified npm run security:kses-corpus and npm run security:kses-corpus:check.

title: WPHX-303.06 — Promote first pure formatting helpers to Haxe parity candidates
description: After the WPHX-303.02 fixture harness exists, move the smallest pure formatting helpers such as zeroise, sanitize_key, sanitize_hex_color, and wp_basename into Haxe parity candidates with oracle receipts and no public ABI drift.
acceptance_criteria: Selected pure formatting helpers have typed Haxe implementations or parity candidates, generated PHP keeps WordPress-compatible public signatures, oracle fixtures pass, and ownership manifests record smell fixes or native boundaries with removal gates.
close_reason: Promoted zeroise, sanitize_key, sanitize_hex_color, and sanitize_hex_color_no_hash into typed Haxe parity candidates. The generated candidate keeps WordPress global PHP function signatures, preserves the sanitize_key apply_filters native boundary, compares 25 formatting/escaping cases against the WordPress 7.0 oracle on local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5, records haxe_parity_candidate ownership, and documents wp_basename plus distribution-surface gaps for WPHX-303 follow-up.

title: WPHX-303.05 — Build KSES differential fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-303.01 surface manifest to build KSES oracle fixtures for wp_kses, wp_kses_post, allowed HTML contexts, bad protocol handling, entity normalization, malformed attributes, and the SimplePie sanitizer bridge.
acceptance_criteria: KSES fixtures compare the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle for allow-list contexts, protocol stripping, entity normalization, attribute parsing, and feed sanitizer behavior; manifest and receipt record security-sensitive gaps before Haxe implementation.
close_reason: Added WPHX-303.05 KSES oracle/candidate fixture harness covering allow-list contexts, protocol stripping, entity normalization, attribute parsing, CSS filtering, and the SimplePie KSES bridge; verified with npm run wp:core:wphx-303-kses and npm run wp:core:wphx-303-kses:check.

title: WPHX-303.04 — Build deprecation and native error signaling fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-303.01 surface manifest to build differential fixtures for wp_trigger_error, _deprecated_function, _deprecated_argument, _doing_it_wrong, and wp_die handler behavior while preserving native PHP warning/deprecation/error semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures compare WordPress 7.0 native warning, notice, deprecation, E_USER_ERROR, hook, output, and wp_die handler behavior; manifest and receipt record any unavoidable PHP-native boundary and Haxe ownership state.
close_reason: Built the deprecation/native error signaling differential harness. The fixture mirrors WordPress 7.0 oracle and candidate source trees, covers wp_trigger_error warning/notice/deprecation/WP_Exception paths, suppression filters, deprecated_function/deprecated_argument/_doing_it_wrong hooks and native emissions, WP_Error wp_die input processing, default/Ajax/JSON wp_die handler selection, and direct default/json/ajax/scalar handler output across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Ownership remains external_oracle with PHP-native trigger_error, WP_Exception, hook/filter, header/output, and die/exit boundaries recorded.

title: WPHX-303.03 — Build WP_Error parity fixture and typed source candidate
description: Use the WPHX-303.01 surface manifest to build WP_Error and is_wp_error oracle fixtures, then prepare the first typed Haxe source candidate for the error object behavior without changing public PHP object shape.
acceptance_criteria: WP_Error fixtures cover constructor, add/remove, code/message/data ordering, additional_data history, public properties, and is_wp_error; typed Haxe candidate records any native PHP object-shape boundary and removal gate with receipts.
close_reason: Built the WP_Error parity candidate harness and typed Haxe runtime helper. The generated candidate keeps the WP_Error PHP class, public errors/error_data arrays, protected additional_data, AllowDynamicProperties, method signatures, wp_error_added action dispatch, and native is_wp_error() boundary; 13 fixture cases cover constructor behavior, add/remove ordering, data history, public property mutation, merge/export, reflection shape, and is_wp_error action behavior across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5.

title: WPHX-303.02 — Build escaping/sanitization differential fixture harness
description: Use the WPHX-303.01 surface manifest to build the first escaping and sanitization oracle fixture harness for esc_html, esc_attr, esc_url, esc_js, sanitize_text_field, and related invalid UTF-8/entity/filter-hook behavior before Haxe implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture harness compares escaping and sanitization seed cases against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned runtime expectations; manifest and receipt identify covered symbols, source units, native boundaries, and remaining gaps.
close_reason: Built the WPHX-303.02 escaping/sanitization differential harness. It mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 source into oracle and candidate roots, covers 16 escaping/sanitization symbols with 20 seed cases, compares local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5 runtimes, and records native option/cache/filter/KSES boundaries plus remaining Haxe-candidate and full-phpunit gaps.

title: WPHX-303.01 — Inventory error/formatting/escaping/KSES surface
description: Build the first WPHX-303 domain surface manifest from the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle and existing ABI/test inventories. The manifest should enumerate relevant source units, public symbols, upstream tests, risk categories, and first differential fixture targets before implementation logic moves into Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic WPHX-303.01 tool generates a manifest and receipt for error/deprecation/formatting/escaping/KSES surface coverage; dotted refs validate; manifest identifies source units, ABI symbols, upstream tests, fixture seeds, ownership state, and follow-up port slices; checks and receipt validation pass.
close_reason: Generated the WPHX-303.01 domain surface manifest, external-oracle ownership record, and receipt from WordPress 7.0 source/test/ABI inventories; identified 200 public symbols, 75 upstream tests, first fixture targets, deferred cross-domain units, and filed WPHX-303.02 through WPHX-303.06 follow-up slices.

title: WPHX-303 — Error, deprecation, formatting, escaping, KSES
description: Port the WordPress error/deprecation/formatting/escaping/KSES domain in behavior-first source-unit slices. Start by inventorying the relevant functions/files and building differential fixtures against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle before moving implementation logic into typed Haxe.
acceptance_criteria: Security, formatting, escaping, KSES, error, and deprecation fixtures compare against the WordPress 7.0 oracle across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; source-unit ownership manifests and receipts record which behavior is Haxe-owned, which PHP-native ABI boundaries remain, and any smell fixes with compatibility evidence.
notes: WPHX-303.02 added an escaping/sanitization differential harness: oracle and candidate source mirrors are generated from WordPress 7.0, 16 public symbols are covered by 20 seed cases, and comparisons pass on local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Native boundaries are explicit: blog_charset via option/cache seed, plugin filter callbacks, and KSES protocol guards. WPHX-303.06 is now unblocked for selected pure formatting Haxe parity candidates.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-303 domain slice: surface inventory, escaping/sanitization fixture, WP_Error typed parity candidate, deprecation/native error signaling fixture, KSES fixture, and selected pure formatting Haxe parity candidates all pass local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP runtime checks. Recorded external-oracle and haxe_parity_candidate ownership manifests, parent closure receipt, and WPHX-700.01 follow-up for broader KSES security/block/feed integration coverage.

title: WPHX-017 — Reconcile WordPress PHP core external refs
description: The hook deepening work after WPHX-302 used WPHX-303 through WPHX-306, but the PRD reserves those refs for later WordPress PHP core domains. Reclassify those hook-hardening issues and evidence under WPHX-302.x dotted refs, allow dotted refs where schemas validate issue refs, and create the real next PRD WPHX-303 domain task so WPHX-300 can continue without numbering drift.
acceptance_criteria: Hook-hardening Beads issues and generated evidence use WPHX-302.x refs; WPHX-303 through WPHX-306 are no longer occupied by hook-subtask meanings; schema/receipt validation supports dotted WPHX refs; the real WPHX-303 Error/deprecation/formatting/escaping/KSES domain task exists under WPHX-300 with appropriate blockers; checks and receipt evidence pass.
close_reason: Reclassified hook-hardening work under WPHX-302.01 through WPHX-302.04, restored real PRD WPHX-303 as the error/deprecation/formatting/escaping/KSES domain task, updated dotted-ref validators and seed ordering, regenerated hook evidence, and passed hook, Beads, inventory, PHP conformance, Haxe formatting, and escape-hatch gates.

title: WPHX-302.04 — Verify hook workset as Haxe-owned distribution surface
description: Promote the hook/plugin API workset from parity-candidate shell emission to verified Haxe-owned distribution surface, removing or explicitly approving any remaining source-transform/runtime-delegate bridges and proving the emitted plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php are stable, idiomatic, reflection-compatible, and plugin-compatible without relying on broad upstream shell transformation.
acceptance_criteria: The hook/plugin API ownership manifests can move to verified_haxe_owned; WPHX-302 through WPHX-305 gates pass; any remaining PHP-native callback/reference/global boundaries are explicitly approved as public ABI boundaries rather than temporary bridges; generated plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php are distribution-ready with source maps/provenance and no broad source-transform bridge.
notes: Renumbered by WPHX-017 from WPHX-306 to WPHX-302.04 so PRD domain ref WPHX-306 remains available for Users/roles/capabilities/auth/cookies/nonces. Historical close text may mention the former ref.
close_reason: WPHX-306 promoted the hook/plugin API workset to verified_haxe_owned. Added the distribution-surface gate, moved WPHX-302 through WPHX-305 ownership manifests out of temporary bridge/parity state, recorded generated plugin.php/class-wp-hook.php provenance and source maps, approved PHP-native callback/reference/global/reflection/include boundaries as public ABI, and verified WPHX-302 through WPHX-306 plus CI conformance.

title: WPHX-302.03 — Move hook public shell emission to Haxe/compiler-owned output
description: Eliminate the remaining JS-authored PHP hook shell template by moving plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php public shell emission into Haxe-owned source, a macro/emitter, a generic Haxe PHP backend improvement, or an ADR-approved Reflaxe/custom PHP target. Preserve WordPress 7.0 public PHP interfaces, native callbacks, by-reference behavior, globals, reflection signatures, and idiomatic generated PHP for existing plugins.
acceptance_criteria: The hook public shell is no longer authored as a broad JavaScript PHP template; generated plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php remain idiomatic and reflection-compatible; WPHX-302 surface, WPHX-303 parity-candidate, and WPHX-304 runtime-boundary gates pass across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; any compiler/custom-target path is backed by compiler-pressure evidence and an ADR.
notes: Renumbered by WPHX-017 from WPHX-305 to WPHX-302.03 so PRD domain ref WPHX-305 remains available for wpdb/database abstraction. Historical close text may mention the former ref.
close_reason: WPHX-305 replaced the broad JS-authored hook public shell template with a source-transform emitter over the locked WordPress plugin.php/class-wp-hook.php oracles, added the shell-emitter gate and CI coverage, and verified WPHX-302/303/304/305 hook checks plus PHP conformance.

title: WPHX-302.02 — Install typed Haxe hook runtime boundary
description: Install a typed Haxe runtime boundary for the generated hook/plugin API shell, moving scalar/string hook decisions into Haxe-owned source while preserving WordPress 7.0 public PHP interfaces, native callback/reference behavior, globals, reflection signatures, and plugin compatibility. Remaining broad public shell emission is intentionally left to WPHX-305.
acceptance_criteria: plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php generated shells delegate bounded decisions to typed Haxe runtime code without broad php.Syntax.code shell bodies; WPHX-302 hook surface, WPHX-303 parity-candidate, and WPHX-304 runtime-boundary gates pass across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; unavoidable native PHP operations are isolated as documented WPHX-211-style boundaries; WPHX-305 owns moving the remaining shell emission into Haxe/compiler-owned output.
notes: Renumbered by WPHX-017 from WPHX-304 to WPHX-302.02 so PRD domain ref WPHX-304 remains available for Options/transients/object cache. Historical close text may mention the former ref.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-304 as the typed Haxe hook runtime-boundary slice. Added src/wphx/wp/hooks/HookRuntime.hx and exposed it through the F7 HookKernel facade; generated plugin.php/class-wp-hook.php now delegate priority normalization, counter increments, dispatch arity, filter/action value-write decisions, default action args, plugin basename trimming, lifecycle hook names, and plugin realpath decisions to typed Haxe while keeping PHP-native callbacks, references, globals, reflection-visible declarations, and include timing intact. Verified npm run php:facade:f7:check, wp:hooks:surface:check, wp:hooks:parity-candidate:check, wp:hooks:runtime-boundary:check, ci:php-conformance:check, and haxe:escape-hatches:check. WPHX-305 owns moving the remaining public shell emission out of the JS PHP template into Haxe/compiler-owned output.

title: WPHX-302.01 — Promote hook decision model to Haxe parity candidate
description: Promote typed Haxe hook decision/model logic to a parity candidate for priority ordering/removal, stack/current hook checks, filter/action counters, callback arity decisions, and plugin basename/lifecycle hook names. The public plugin.php/class-wp-hook.php shell remains the WPHX-302 bridge until WPHX-304 replaces PHP-observable callback/reference/global behavior without broad php.Syntax.code bodies.
acceptance_criteria: A haxe_parity_candidate ownership manifest exists for the hook decision model; the typed Haxe candidate is compared against vanilla WordPress 7.0 behavior across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; WPHX-302 hook surface still passes; no broad PHP shell bodies are pasted into Haxe strings; WPHX-304 owns remaining public shell replacement.
notes: Renumbered by WPHX-017 from WPHX-303 to WPHX-302.01 so PRD domain ref WPHX-303 remains available for Error/deprecation/formatting/escaping/KSES. Historical close text may mention the former ref.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-303 as a typed Haxe hook decision-model parity candidate. Added fixtures/wp-hooks HookCandidateKernel with no new Dynamic/untyped/cast/php.Syntax.code usage, plus npm run wp:hooks:parity-candidate comparing priority ordering/removal, stack/current filter checks, counters, dispatch arity/value-write decisions, default action args, and plugin basename/lifecycle hook names against vanilla WordPress 7.0 across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. WPHX-302 hook surface still passes with 56/56 symbols; WPHX-302 bridge removal now points at WPHX-304 for idiomatic public shell replacement.

title: WPHX-302 — Hooks/plugin API
description: Port and validate the WordPress hooks/plugin API domain in small source-unit slices, starting from plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php fixture parity.
acceptance_criteria: Complete hook API surface and fixture-plugin parity are recorded against the WordPress 7.0 oracle, with typed Haxe ownership manifests and receipts for plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php slices.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-302 hooks/plugin API surface: npm run wp:hooks:surface:check passes with 56/56 ABI symbols covered for plugin.php and class-wp-hook.php; generated hook shell and copied WordPress 7.0 oracle match for deprecated hooks, ref-array actions, remove-all helpers, plugin path helpers, activation/deactivation hooks, and uninstall hook registration across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Ownership is recorded as a bounded temporary bridge in manifests/ownership/wphx-302-hooks-workset.v1.json, with WPHX-303 opened to promote the hook bridge toward a Haxe parity candidate. Receipt: receipts/wp-hooks/wphx-302-hook-surface.v1.json.

title: WPHX-301 — Bootstrap, load order, constants, environment
description: Port the WordPress bootstrap/load-order/constants/environment domain in small source-unit slices, beginning with a deterministic trace harness for normal, SHORTINIT, install, and recovery-mode bootstrap paths.
acceptance_criteria: Normal, SHORTINIT, install, and recovery bootstrap traces compare against the WordPress 7.0 oracle with debug and parity build profiles, and each Haxe-owned source unit records ownership and receipt evidence.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-301 bootstrap trace scaffold: npm run wp:bootstrap:trace:check passes; normal, SHORTINIT, install, and recovery scenario gates from the typed Haxe fixture match the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle markers, with ordered include/load phase digests recorded for wp-load.php/wp-settings.php and scaffolded ownership captured in manifests/ownership/wphx-301-bootstrap-workset.v1.json. Receipt: receipts/wp-bootstrap/wphx-301-bootstrap-traces.v1.json.

title: WPHX-300 — WordPress PHP core
description: Port WordPress first-party PHP runtime logic for the 7.0 line in behavior-first domain slices, using the PHP ABI/runtime/linker foundation from WPHX-200.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX-301 through WPHX-322 are complete or explicitly split into verified source-unit tasks with parity receipts.

title: WPHX-211 — Audit existing Dynamic escape hatches
description: Audit Haxe Dynamic/untyped/raw target escape hatches that existed before the hard rule. Narrow each use to concrete Haxe or target-native types where possible; where Dynamic remains required, isolate it behind a named boundary typedef or helper and add a nearby justification comment.
acceptance_criteria: All tracked Haxe Dynamic usages are either replaced with narrower types or documented as minimal runtime/compiler boundaries with a removal/narrowing condition.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-211 escape hatch audit: npm run haxe:escape-hatches:check passes; tracked Haxe Dynamic/untyped/raw target syntax/cast/Any usages are narrowed or have nearby WPHX-211 justifications, with PHP callable/native value and macro JSON boundaries isolated behind named typedefs. Receipt: receipts/haxe/wphx-211-escape-hatches.v1.json.

title: WPHX-208 — PHP compiler conformance CI
description: Turn the PHP target fixtures and ABI/linker conformance checks into a required CI matrix for compiler/runtime-sensitive changes.
acceptance_criteria: The matrix runs on every target/runtime change.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-208 PHP compiler conformance CI: npm run ci:php-conformance:check passes; .github/workflows/php-conformance.yml defines a GitHub Actions matrix for compiler/runtime-sensitive path changes, restores the pinned WordPress 7.0 oracle checkout, warms pinned PHP 8.4/8.5 Docker images, and runs the PHP smoke/facade, ABI/macro, boundary/facade/linker/public-type/debug source-map suites. Receipt: receipts/ci/wphx-208-php-conformance-ci.v1.json.

title: WPHX-206 — Public class/interface/trait emission
description: Emit public WordPress class, interface, and trait shells with native names and reflection-compatible contracts.
acceptance_criteria: The exact public class/interface/trait fixture suite is green.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-206 public class/interface/trait emission: npm run wp:public-types:check passes; generated WordPress-shaped shell preserves global and namespaced PHP public type identity, repeated include safety, reflection-visible class/interface/trait contracts, and Haxe-delegated method payloads across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Receipt: receipts/wp-public-types/wphx-206-public-types.v1.json.

title: WPHX-205 — Original-path shell and file-segment linker
description: Build the deterministic file linker that emits original-path PHP shells and maps generated Haxe segments into the WordPress distribution layout.
acceptance_criteria: Load and template fixtures pass from the generated distribution layout.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-205 original-path shell and file-segment linker: npm run wp:linker:check passes; deterministic linker emits a combined WordPress-shaped generated root from ordered file segments, and include/load plus template/caller-scope fixtures match the PHP oracles across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Receipt: receipts/wp-linker/wphx-205-original-path-linker.v1.json.

title: WPHX-204 — Deterministic global facade generator
description: Generate original-name PHP global function facades from the ABI manifest and typed Haxe bindings.
acceptance_criteria: Generated wrappers reproduce the ABI manifest deterministically.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-204 deterministic global facade generator: npm run wp:facade:globals:check passes; generated original-name PHP wrappers for add_filter, apply_filters, and _wp_array_set from the WordPress ABI manifest plus typed @:wp.global Haxe bindings; PHP reflection matches ABI projection for defaults, variadics, and by-reference parameters across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Receipt: receipts/wp-facade/wphx-204-global-facades.v1.json.

title: WPHX-203 — Native WordPress PHP boundary types
description: Codify native PHP boundary types for WordPress arrays, globals, callables, references, and WP_Error-style values.
acceptance_criteria: Conformance tests cover arrays, globals, callables, references, and errors.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-203 native WordPress PHP boundary types: npm run wp:boundary:check passes; generated Haxe-backed shell matches PHP oracle for native arrays, globals, callables, by-reference parameters/returns/callbacks, and WP_Error-style native values across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Receipt: receipts/wp-boundary/wphx-203-boundary-types.v1.json.

title: WPHX-202 — @:wp.* macro contract and compile-time validator
description: Define the typed Haxe annotations and macro validation contract that binds Haxe declarations to WordPress ABI manifest entries.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid bindings fail compilation with deterministic diagnostics.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP — Add module-function original-path adapters for feed embed HTTPS helpers
description:
WPHX-312.101 coverage found WPHX-312.04 still depends on copied WordPress public PHP for feed.php, embed.php, class-wp-oembed.php, class-wp-embed.php, https-detection.php, and https-migration.php helper behavior. Move this compiler pressure into WPHX PHP rather than extending copied oracle fixtures: add minimized module-function/class adapter IR support for the covered symbols, emit original-path public adapters, and keep the existing WPHX-312.04 oracle fixture plus installed/feed/oEmbed/HTTPS gates as behavior evidence until replacement passes.

Covered source pressure includes module-level functions, class methods, global state, hooks/filters, conditional behavior, XML/html output helpers, HTTPS option migration, and local HTTPS detection boundaries. This must remain generic WPHX PHP compiler/WordPress-profile work, not runner-patched public PHP.
acceptance_criteria: Minimized compiler fixtures cover module-level public PHP function adapter emission for representative feed/embed/HTTPS helpers; WPHX PHP emits original-path adapters for selected WPHX-312.04 symbols or records narrower blockers; the WPHX-312.04 oracle fixture, public-shell snapshots, receipts, and adoption checks remain green; copied public PHP remains non-claimed until replacement gates pass.
close_reason: Added selected WPHX PHP module-function original-path adapter gate for WPHX-312.04 feed helpers. The new runner emits unguarded wp-includes/feed.php get_default_feed() and feed_content_type( = '') adapters, compares against a minimized WordPress 7.0 oracle, updates public-shell snapshots/adoption CI, and records receipt:wphx-comp-php-module-function-adapters. title: WPHX-314.17 — Close bounded blocks/interactivity PHP domain
description: Final WPHX-314 closure should happen only after the remaining evidence gates are resolved. This issue records the bounded blocks/interactivity PHP domain-closure receipt, updates the progress matrix, and closes the parent only if evidence supports the bounded non-claims.
acceptance_criteria: All WPHX-314 child gates are closed or explicitly superseded; a domain-closure receipt summarizes surface inventory, adapter-contract candidates, oracle/candidate fixtures, installed gates, upstream PHPUnit status, public-shell ownership state, Haxe-owned candidate promotions, and bounded non-claims; docs/operations/progress-matrix.md changes WPHX-314 from active to closed only with evidence; parent wordpresshx-l76.20 is closed after receipts and Beads export are current.

title: WPHX-314.15 — Promote selected block PHP decisions to Haxe candidates
description: Current WPHX-314 evidence includes a typed adapter-contract candidate and many copied-oracle fixtures, but broad runtime logic remains copied upstream PHP bridge evidence. This issue promotes selected bounded block PHP decisions into typed Haxe parity candidates where evidence is strong enough, starting with low-risk parser/render/support/style/interactivity decisions rather than whole-file ownership.
acceptance_criteria: At least one bounded WPHX-314 behavior cluster moves from copied-oracle bridge evidence to typed Haxe-owned candidate source with deterministic oracle/candidate comparison; public PHP ABI replacement remains explicit if not generated; manifests, ownership receipts, and progress matrix identify claimed Haxe ownership and remaining copied/public-boundary behavior.

title: WPHX-314.14 — Expand installed block rendering coverage
description: WPHX-314.12 adds a packaged installed-style block rendering gate for selected cases, but the progress matrix still leaves broader installed block rendering, database-backed behavior, template/theme integration, REST insertion, and ecosystem block routes unclaimed. This issue expands installed-style oracle/candidate coverage without claiming Gutenberg browser/package ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Installed-style coverage exercises representative front-end and REST/admin-adjacent block rendering paths across parser/render, supports/bindings, patterns/hooks, style engine, selected core block renderers, HTML API, and interactivity directives; database-backed and theme/template interactions are covered or explicitly deferred with cross-domain owners; receipts state covered behavior and remaining non-claims.

title: WPHX-314.13 — Unblock blocks/interactivity upstream PHPUnit ratchet
description: WPHX-314.11 declares selected upstream PHPUnit groups for blocks, style engine, HTML API, and interactivity, but the current ratchet status is blocked on missing local PHPUnit runtime inputs. This issue provisions or documents the required vanilla/candidate PHPUnit roots, wp-tests-config.php files, isolated databases, and candidate package inputs for the WPHX-314 groups so the ratchet can record executable pass/pass or owned deltas.
acceptance_criteria: npm run upstream:phpunit-ratchet:provision:check and npm run upstream:phpunit-ratchet:check cover the WPHX-314 blocks-parser-render-core, blocks-supports-bindings-core, blocks-hooks-patterns-core, style-engine-core, html-api-core, and interactivity-api-core groups with non-blocked results; any candidate failures have known-delta records with owner and expiry; receipts and progress matrix distinguish pass/pass parity from blocked/runtime-input status.

title: WPHX-314.16 — Audit and retire blocks/interactivity public PHP shell gaps
description: WPHX-314 fixtures are mostly copied-oracle or packaged bridge evidence and do not claim generated original-path public PHP ownership. This issue audits WPHX-314 public PHP boundaries and retires copied, generated-temporary, runner-patched, or non-original-path shells where WPHX PHP Adapter IR or backend features can support the boundary.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable audit lists every WPHX-314 public PHP boundary and ownership state; eligible copied/generated-temporary/runner-patched shells are replaced by compiler-emitted original-path adapters with unsupported=[] receipts, or linked to compiler-pressure issues with minimized blockers and removal gates; docs distinguish durable generated public PHP ownership from copied-oracle fixture evidence.

title: WPHX-312.96 — Close bounded HTTP, cron, mail, feed, and embed domain
description: Final WPHX-312 closure should happen only after the remaining audit gates are resolved. This issue records the domain-closure receipt for the bounded HTTP/cron/mail/feed/embed slice, updates the progress matrix, and closes the parent only if evidence supports the bounded non-claims.
acceptance_criteria: All WPHX-312 child gates are closed or explicitly superseded; a domain-closure receipt summarizes surface inventory, adapter-contract candidates, oracle/candidate fixtures, installed gates, upstream PHPUnit status, public-shell ownership state, and bounded non-claims; docs/operations/progress-matrix.md changes WPHX-312 from active to closed only with evidence; parent wordpresshx-l76.18 is closed after receipts and Beads export are current.
close_reason: Added WPHX-312 domain-closure receipt and updated progress matrix. Closure summarizes surface inventory, adapter-contract evidence, oracle/candidate fixtures, WPHX PHP HTTP candidates, HTTP transport parity, cron/mail transport, installed feed/embed/widget/privacy routes, public-shell audit, copied-surface plan, and WPHX-312 upstream PHPUnit pass/pass classifications while preserving bounded non-claims.

title: WPHX-312.95 — Audit and retire remaining HTTP/feed/embed public PHP shell gaps
description: The WPHX-312 closure audit found that many request/helper candidates now use WPHX compiler-emitted original-path shells, but several bounded candidates and copied-oracle fixtures still explicitly do not claim durable generated public PHP replacement. This issue audits the WPHX-312 manifests/receipts for copied, generated-temporary, runner-patched, or non-original-path public PHP shells and retires them where the current Adapter IR/WPHX PHP lane can support the boundary.
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable audit lists every WPHX-312 public PHP boundary and its ownership state; remaining copied/generated-temporary/runner-patched shells are either replaced by compiler-emitted original-path adapters with unsupported=[] receipts or linked to follow-up compiler-pressure issues with minimized blockers and removal gates; progress matrix and compiler docs distinguish durable generated public PHP ownership from copied-oracle fixture evidence.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-312 public PHP shell gap audit. The audit classifies 89 ownership manifests, records 12 durable generated shells, 16 WPHX-backed ownership-state reconciliation items, 13 remaining WP_Http::request branch shell gaps, and 46 copied-oracle public surfaces, with unresolved debt linked to WPHX-312.98, WPHX-312.99, and WPHX-312.100.

title: WPHX-312.94 — Expand installed feed, embed, widget, and privacy routes
description: WPHX-312 has many copied-oracle and packaged installed-style route checks, but the progress matrix still leaves full installed feed routing/rendering, installed oEmbed/post embed rendering, installed privacy request confirmation/management routing, admin privacy list-table rendering, and RSS widget/sidebar/block routes unclaimed. This issue turns those remaining installed routes into explicit evidence or scoped deferrals.
acceptance_criteria: Oracle/candidate installed-style coverage exercises representative front-end feed routes, oEmbed/embed routes, RSS widget/sidebar or block rendering routes, and privacy request confirmation/management/admin-list-table routes; cross-domain ownership with WPHX-315/WPHX-316/WPHX-314 is documented where appropriate; receipts state what is covered versus still deferred.
close_reason: Added WPHX-312.94 feed/embed/widget/privacy installed-route gate covering RSS2 feeds, comments feeds, oEmbed REST, post embeds, RSS widget/sidebar, RSS block, privacy confirmation, and privacy admin list-table routes; refreshed public-shell audit and copied-surface plan.

title: WPHX-312.97 — Unblock HTTP/cron/mail/feed/embed upstream PHPUnit ratchet
description: Audit of WPHX-312 found that WPHX-312.10 declares the selected upstream PHPUnit groups, but the current run is still deterministically blocked on missing local runtime inputs. This issue exists to provision or document the required vanilla/candidate PHPUnit roots, wp-tests-config.php files, isolated databases, and candidate package inputs for the WPHX-312 groups so the ratchet can classify real pass/pass or deltas.
acceptance_criteria: npm run upstream:phpunit-ratchet:provision:check and npm run upstream:phpunit-ratchet:check cover the WPHX-312 http-cron-mail-core, feed-simplepie-core, oembed-embed-core, and https-privacy-request-core groups with non-blocked results; any candidate failures have known-delta records with owner and expiry; receipts and progress matrix distinguish pass/pass parity from blocked/runtime-input status.
close_reason: Unblocked WPHX-312 upstream PHPUnit ratchet. Corrected the WPHX-312 HTTP group selector by removing non-runnable tests/phpunit/tests/http/base.php, provisioned the disposable PHPUnit environment, and recorded 62 vanilla/candidate runs with all four WPHX-312 groups classifying pass/pass and no blocked runtime inputs.

title: WPHX-312.92 — Add live or recorded HTTP transport parity gate
description: WPHX-312 has deterministic fake/stub HTTP coverage and many bounded WP_Http request candidates, but the closure audit found that live Requests transport behavior, redirect/network/proxy/TLS behavior, timeout races, and streaming transfer behavior remain explicitly unclaimed. This issue covers the next network-side-effect gate without broadening to full WP_Http ownership prematurely.
acceptance_criteria: A live or recorded transport parity runner compares vanilla and candidate behavior for representative WP_Http/wp_remote_request paths including success, redirects, errors, headers/cookies, proxy/TLS relevant options, and streaming or response-size behavior; network nondeterminism is controlled through fixtures or recordings; manifests and receipts state which behavior is owned, which remains PHP/public-boundary, and which live transport cases remain deferred.
close_reason: Added recorded HTTP transport parity gate comparing vanilla WP_Http::request against WPHX PHP compiler-emitted original-path request shell for seven deterministic Requests-boundary cases covering success/defaults, preempt, invalid/blocked errors, streaming, headers/cookies/SSL/body/redirect/response-size, proxy auth/TLS handoff, and exception/nonblocking behavior. Added manifests, ownership, receipt, npm scripts, refreshed public-shell audit, and updated progress matrix without claiming live network I/O or whole-file WP_Http ownership.

title: WPHX-312.93 — Add real cron and mail transport installed gate
description: Existing WPHX-312 fixtures cover cron scheduling/dispatch decisions and PHPMailer setup with no-delivery or deterministic stubs. The closure audit found that real loopback cron transport, production timing races, and real PHP mail/SMTP transport remain unclaimed. This issue adds an installed-style gate for those side effects or records bounded deferrals with evidence.
acceptance_criteria: An installed-style oracle/candidate gate exercises cron spawn/dispatch through a real or controlled loopback path and wp_mail/PHPMailer through a real controlled transport or explicit local capture transport; timing, lock, retry, and failure observations are recorded; receipts identify any side effects still deferred and why they are outside the current WPHX-312 closure boundary.
close_reason: Added controlled cron/mail transport installed-style gate. The runner compares oracle/candidate package roots for spawn_cron controlled local HTTP loopback success, lock-held suppression, loopback connection failure, wp_mail/PHPMailer SMTP capture success, and SMTP connection failure. Manifests and receipts document remaining deferrals for production cron timing races, persistent database/object-cache locks, PHP mail delivery, authenticated/TLS SMTP, DNS/MX behavior, and generated public PHP replacement.
