title: Remove dead WordPress adapter template renderer
description:
Why this issue exists: The WordPress adapter profile now has zero PhpRawBlock occurrences and zero active adapter templates, but WphxPhpWordPressAdapters.hx still carries the old renderTemplate/templateError helper path and imports, and the raw-block guard assumes the now-empty template directory exists in the working tree. That leaves dead template infrastructure after the zero-template milestone.

What needs to be done: Remove the unused WordPress-profile template renderer helpers/imports, keep the manifest template-provenance type only where the compiler schema still needs it, make the adapter raw-block/template guard treat a missing template directory as an empty template set, refresh guard outputs/receipts/docs as needed, and keep zero raw-block/template checks green.

Acceptance criteria: No renderTemplate/templateError dead helper remains in WphxPhpWordPressAdapters; adapter raw-block guard passes from a zero-template state without relying on an untracked empty directory; raw-block/template counts remain zero; Haxe formatting, public-shell snapshots if affected, receipts validation, Beads validation, and precommit pass.
design: Do not reintroduce templates or raw PHP bodies. This is cleanup after IR promotion, not a change to emitted PHP behavior or a claim over broader WordPress runtime ownership.
close_reason: Removed the obsolete WordPress-profile renderTemplate/templateError helper path, made the adapter raw-block guard treat a missing template directory as an empty template set, refreshed the zero raw-block/template policy evidence, and kept focused checks plus precommit green.

Remove the get-first template from the active WordPress adapter template set, update the WPHX-312.66 transport candidate to require template absence and IR feature evidence for both transport helpers, refresh snapshots/guards/docs/progress matrix/receipts, and keep behavior parity against the six transport oracle/candidate cases.
close_reason: Promoted WP_Http::_get_first_available_transport from a compiler-owned raw adapter template to structured WPHX PHP core IR, removed its template, refreshed transport candidate/snapshot/adapter-template guard evidence, and documented the active template count drop to two.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP — promote dispatch adapter template to IR

Add only the generic IR/printer surface needed for this body, such as method-scope static locals, remove the dispatch template from the active WordPress adapter template set, update transport candidate/snapshot evidence, refresh docs/progress matrix/receipts, and keep the raw-block/template guards green. This reduces the durable raw target-native adapter surface instead of normalizing templates as the long-term destination.
close_reason: Promoted WP_Http::_dispatch_request from a compiler-owned raw adapter template to structured WPHX PHP core IR, added static-local/dynamic-new IR support, refreshed transport candidate/snapshots/guards, and documented the promotion.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP-PROFILE-ADAPTER-REGISTRY — Move WordPress-specific adapter bodies out of compiler core
description:
Why this issue exists: WPHX PHP currently keeps bounded WordPress adapter bodies such as WP_Http helper adapters inline in src/wphx/compiler/php/WphxPhpCompiler.hx. That is acceptable as compiler-pressure scaffolding while the project proves PHP IR semantics, but it couples the compiler core to WordPress helper names and can calcify into a backend design smell.

What needs to be done: Introduce a profile/adapter registry boundary so generic PHP IR lowering stays in the compiler core while WordPress-specific adapter plans live in a dedicated WordPress profile module or generated adapter-plan layer. Preserve existing manifests, snapshots, and grouped WP_Http behavior while moving wp-http-* adapter body construction behind that boundary.

Non-goals: do not redesign the full custom PHP backend, do not remove WordPress profile support, and do not block current WP_Http shell retirement slices.
acceptance_criteria: WPHX PHP still passes grouped WP_Http helpers, public-shell snapshots, receipts validation, and Beads validation after WordPress-specific adapter bodies move behind a profile/registry boundary; compiler core retains generic PHP IR/printer behavior without direct WP_Http method-specific body construction.
close_reason: WordPress-specific wp-http-* method body construction now lives in WphxPhpWordPressAdapters.hx, while WphxPhpCompiler.hx retains generic PHP IR, feature recording, file planning, bootstrap, manifest writing, and printing; grouped WP_Http, buildCookieHeader, processHeaders, and public-shell snapshot checks pass.

title: Clarify staged WPHX PHP custom compiler strategy
description:
Why this issue exists: Recent compiler strategy discussion clarified that WPHX PHP is the active custom compiler lane, staged through Adapter IR and WordPress original-path public adapters first, not a rejection of a future full custom/reflaxe.php backend. Existing docs still contain wording that can be read as 'no custom compiler' rather than 'custom compiler, staged and evidence-driven'.

What needs to be done: Update ADR/docs/progress/agent instruction surfaces to state that WPHX PHP is the in-repo custom compiler track with a reusable modern PHP core plus WordPress profile. Preserve the current near-term rule that stock Haxe PHP remains a borrowable private implementation/stdphp oracle and that WPHX PHP must not jump to arbitrary-Haxe backend scope without fixture pressure. Record a receipt and keep progress claims unchanged.

Acceptance criteria: ADR-013/ADR-001 or operations compiler docs distinguish staged custom compiler lane from a full arbitrary backend flip; AGENTS/docs rules direct future work to design Haxe ABI metadata and Adapter IR as if a full custom compiler will consume them later; progress matrix reflects the clarification without score changes; receipts and Beads validation pass.
close_reason: Clarified WPHX PHP as the active staged custom PHP compiler lane across ADR-001, ADR-013, compiler operations docs, agent instructions, oracle summary, progress matrix, persistent memory, and an operations receipt while preserving the current Adapter IR-backed public-adapter scope and non-claiming a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.

title: Emit non-throwing Haxe PHP bootstrap profile option
description:
Why this issue exists: WPHX-COMP-PHP-BOOTSTRAP-ERROR-HANDLER-PROBE proves stock Haxe PHP bootstrap installs a throwing error handler by default when no previous handler exists, while defining HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER=true before php\Boot::__hx__init() preserves PHP warning return behavior. WordPress-compatible broad public-shell profiles need the safer behavior emitted by default.

What needs to be done: Add a WPHX PHP profile/bootstrap option that defines HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER before php\Boot::__hx__init() for WordPress parity/release public shells, while documenting/debugging any profile that intentionally keeps Haxe's handler.

Acceptance criteria: Generated bootstrap shape includes the non-throwing policy where selected; existing bounded fixtures are updated or explicitly opted out; warning/error-handler probe covers the emitted profile path; docs, manifests, and receipts are updated.
close_reason: Implemented the WPHX PHP WordPress-profile non-throwing bootstrap policy by emitting HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER before php.Boot initialization, kept an explicit stock-control policy, refreshed bootstrap/snapshot receipts, and documented the closed profile gate.

title: Add Haxe PHP bootstrap stack-trace/source-map probe
description:
Why this issue exists: Broad generated public-shell claims need usable stack traces and source-map/source-position behavior when Haxe implementation classes are reached through original-path WPHX adapters.

What needs to be done: Add a minimized debug fixture that triggers a controlled exception through a WPHX public shell into stock Haxe PHP implementation code, records stack frames in debug/parity/release profiles, and checks source-map or normalized source-position policy.

Acceptance criteria: Runner records manifest and receipt; debug/parity profiles show usable source mapping or documented known deltas; release profile behavior is explicit; docs and progress matrix are updated.
close_reason: Added the WPHX PHP bootstrap debug/source-map probe across debug, parity, and release profiles; recorded normalized stack frames, source-map presence policy, source-position mapping, docs, manifest, and receipt.

title: Add Haxe PHP bootstrap include-path/autoload probe
description:
Why this issue exists: ADR-014 requires observable evidence for the Haxe PHP runtime bootstrap before broad WPHX public-shell distribution claims. Include path mutation, SPL autoloader registration order, and repeated bootstrap behavior are PHP request-state effects that plugins and operators can observe.

What needs to be done: Add a minimized WPHX PHP fixture/probe that records include_path before/after generated shell bootstrap, spl_autoload_functions ordering/count before/after repeated require/include, php\Boot::__hx__init availability, and idempotence across multiple original-path shells sharing the same bootstrap constant.

Acceptance criteria: Runner records deterministic manifest and receipt; probe verifies include_path append behavior, autoloader order, repeated bootstrap safety, no duplicate autoloaders for same bootstrap constant, and documents any remaining gap for multiple constants/profiles.
close_reason: Added a minimized WPHX PHP bootstrap include-path/autoload probe with two original-path shells sharing one Haxe bootstrap constant. The runner verifies append-once Haxe library path behavior, appended SPL autoloader order after an existing loader, repeated require idempotence, shared-constant idempotence across shells, php\Boot availability, stock Haxe PHP implementation delegation, docs, manifest, and receipt evidence.

title: Add Haxe PHP warning/error-handler bootstrap probe
description:
Why this issue exists: Stock Haxe PHP php.Boot may install an error handler unless HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER disables it. WordPress warnings/notices are observable and must not silently become exceptions in broad public-shell claims.

What needs to be done: Add a minimized fixture that compares PHP warning/notice behavior before and after Haxe bootstrap with and without HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER, records error_reporting and set_error_handler effects, and defines the release/parity profile policy.

Acceptance criteria: Runner records manifest and receipt; probe distinguishes WordPress-compatible warning behavior from Haxe exception conversion; ADR-014/docs updated if evidence changes the policy.
close_reason: Added the WPHX PHP bootstrap warning/error-handler probe, recorded manifest/receipt evidence for default throwing handler behavior, HAXE_CUSTOM_ERROR_HANDLER preservation, existing-handler preservation, and seeded non-throwing bootstrap profile follow-up.

title: Add copied/JS-patched shell retirement policy
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle stated that copied, transformed, or JS-patched shells can support candidate evidence but cannot support durable public PHP ownership claims. Existing ownership docs describe temporary bridges, but the WPHX PHP path needs explicit shell states and removal gates.

What needs to be done: Add an ADR appendix or new ADR section defining states such as bridge_shell, generated_helper_with_temporary_shell, compiler_emitted_original_path_shell, durable_public_adapter, and whole_file_owned, including allowed claims and required removal gates.

Acceptance criteria: ADR/ownership docs define the shell-retirement states; receipts and progress docs link the policy; future durable public PHP claims are required to cite compiler-emitted original-path shell evidence or a documented backend/custom-target improvement.
close_reason: Defined shell-retirement states and durable public PHP claim gates across ADR-001, ownership docs, compiler docs, agent instructions, progress matrix, and operations receipt.

title: Add WPHX conditional declaration and pluggable timing fixture
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle recommended proving conditional declarations and pluggable-style load timing before broad global-function migration. WordPress function_exists timing is observable and cannot be replaced by class autoloading.

What needs to be done: Add a bounded WPHX PHP fixture for conditional global declarations at an original load point, preferably pluggable.php-style or a small Core function where declaration timing is observable.

Acceptance criteria: The WPHX emitter generates conditional declarations from typed metadata/adapter plan; probes verify function_exists before/after load, repeated require safety, override/predefinition behavior, reflection-visible ABI, manifest unsupported=[], generated-shape evidence, receipt, and docs updates.
close_reason: Added a minimized WPHX PHP pluggable timing fixture that emits guarded original-path wp-includes/pluggable.php declarations from module-level Haxe functions, verifies function_exists timing, repeated require safety, predefinition override behavior, reflection ABI, manifest unsupported=[], docs, and receipt evidence.

title: Add original-path include side-effect fixture before templates
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle recommended a smaller file-level side-effect/include-return slice before mixed PHP/HTML templates. Include-time execution, include return values, include_once behavior, function-scope include behavior, and output buffering are observable WordPress contracts.

What needs to be done: Add a bounded original-path script fixture emitted by WPHX PHP or typed adapter plan that exercises top-level include execution, include return values, repeated include versus include_once, function-scope include behavior, and output buffering if relevant.

Acceptance criteria: Generated original-path script fixture passes PHP lint, static/generated-shape checks, and runtime probes for include timing/return/scope/output behavior; ownership state and removal gates are recorded; docs/receipts update; mixed PHP/HTML template ownership remains explicitly separate.
close_reason: Added WPHX PHP include side-effect script adapter fixture with generated-shape snapshots, runtime include/include_once/function-scope/output probes, manifests, receipts, and docs.

title: Add Haxe PHP bootstrap lifecycle ADR
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle highlighted Haxe PHP runtime bootstrap as observable WordPress request-state behavior. Current WPHX shells call stock Haxe runtime initialization for implementation classes, but broad distribution claims need explicit policy for include path, autoloading, error handling, source maps, stack traces, and repeated include safety.

What needs to be done: Add an ADR documenting when the Haxe runtime bootstraps, autoloader registration order, Haxe error handler policy, warnings/notices behavior, source-map/stack-trace policy, debug vs release behavior, and repeated require/include safety.

Acceptance criteria: ADR is accepted and linked from WPHX PHP compiler docs; it names required gates before broad public-shell distribution claims; it includes follow-up fixtures for include path/autoload/error-handler/stack-trace/source-map observations; receipt and progress docs are refreshed.
close_reason: Accepted ADR-014 for Haxe PHP bootstrap lifecycle in WordPress, linked it from WPHX PHP compiler docs, recorded progress-matrix status, filed follow-up bootstrap probes for include-path/autoload, warning/error-handler, and stack-trace/source-map behavior, and added a compiler-policy receipt.

title: Add WPHX-generated public-shell snapshot lane
description:
Why this issue exists: ADR-003 covers generated-PHP snapshots, and the oracle response says WPHX-generated public shells also need generated-shape snapshots/AST contracts before durable public-shell claims broaden.

What needs to be done: Add snapshot or AST-contract coverage for WPHX PHP emitted shell shapes: global function, public class/interface, protected method, by-reference parameter, conditional declaration, native array mutation, and include/top-level side-effect shell.

Acceptance criteria: A deterministic runner compiles representative WPHX PHP fixtures twice, checks byte stability, runs php -l, verifies selected exact or AST-normalized contracts, records evidence_class=generated_shape and artifact_scope=minimized_fixture/linked_candidate, and updates ADR-003/docs/receipts.
close_reason: Added WPHX PHP public-shell generated-shape snapshot lane covering current shell shapes with deterministic compile-twice checks, php -l, exact/AST contracts, manifest/receipt artifacts, docs, and progress matrix updates.

title: Add WPHX PHP adapter IR and scope ADR
description:
Why this issue exists: The oracle recommended a dedicated ADR that defines the WPHX PHP emitter as a WordPress public adapter emitter, not a general PHP backend. Without that boundary, feature pressure from WP_Http, pluggables, includes, and templates can accidentally grow the emitter into an undeclared backend.

What needs to be done: Add an ADR that defines accepted metadata, adapter IR nodes, feature admission rules, unsupported-construct failure policy, no raw production PHP string policy, ABI-manifest mapping, and escalation rules for stock Haxe PHP improvements versus WPHX emitter features versus custom target work.

Acceptance criteria: ADR is accepted; docs/operations/wphx-php-compiler.md links it; progress matrix records the strategy impact; a receipt links the ADR; the ADR states that WPHX PHP is bounded to original-path WordPress public adapters until evidence justifies a broader backend.
close_reason: Accepted ADR-013 for WPHX PHP Adapter IR and scope, refactored WphxPhpCompiler so existing original-path public shell emission lowers through Adapter IR file/declaration plans before printing PHP, documented the native Haxe PHP source as a borrowable implementation oracle for generic lowering/runtime behavior, updated compiler docs/progress matrix, and added a receipt with verification commands and non-claims.

title: Record oracle response for original-path PHP emission strategy

This must be captured in repo docs, receipts, and Beads so future compiler slices do not rely on chat history.

What needs to be done: Update the oracle review document with a concise response summary and accepted decision, record the strategy in the relevant operations/ADR surfaces, create follow-up Beads issues for the ADR/snapshot/bootstrap/adapter-IR work, and refresh evidence links.

Acceptance criteria: The repo records the oracle recommendation that stock Haxe PHP remains the implementation emitter while WPHX PHP remains the bounded original-path public adapter emitter; the docs distinguish no-fork/no-reflaxe-php-yet from future escalation criteria; follow-up Beads issues exist for the recommended ADR/gate work; receipts and progress docs expose the evidence; validation gates pass.
close_reason: Recorded the oracle response for original-path PHP emission strategy: stock Haxe PHP remains the private implementation emitter, WPHX PHP is the bounded WordPress original-path public adapter emitter, no fork/broad reflaxe.php target is justified yet, ADR/docs/progress/rules were amended, and follow-up Beads issues were seeded for adapter IR, snapshots, bootstrap, shell retirement, buildCookieHeader, processHeaders, conditional declarations, and include side effects.

title: WPHX-COMP-PHP.05 — Reduce grouped WP_Http header-cookie helper blockers
description:
Why this issue exists: WPHX-COMP-PHP.04 proved grouped original-path WPHX PHP emission for public parser helpers plus protected WP_Http::parse_url, but the next neighboring helpers, processHeaders and buildCookieHeader, require richer PHP ABI/lowering support such as by-reference parameters, native PHP arrays, loops, conditionals, object construction, and cookie/header side effects.

What needs to be done: Extend the WPHX PHP emitter with the smallest generic feature needed for a neighboring WP_Http header/cookie helper, or capture a minimized compiler-pressure blocker that explains why processHeaders/buildCookieHeader cannot yet be compiler-emitted. Preserve stock Haxe PHP implementation reuse where practical; keep WordPress-specific lowering named and bounded.

Acceptance criteria: A focused WPHX PHP fixture or minimized blocker covers at least one missing generic feature needed by processHeaders or buildCookieHeader, such as by-reference arguments or native-array/loop lowering; package scripts or receipts expose the evidence; existing grouped WP_Http parser helper checks remain green; docs/progress notes identify the remaining header/cookie gaps and whole-file WP_Http non-claims.
close_reason: WPHX-COMP-PHP.05 adds @:wp.byRef parameter emission in the WPHX PHP emitter, a focused by-reference ABI fixture/runner/receipt, package scripts, and docs/progress notes while keeping the grouped WP_Http parser-helper check green.

What needs to be done: Extend the grouped compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php driver, or reduce generic compiler-pressure blockers, for the next neighboring parser/header helpers such as processHeaders, buildCookieHeader, and protected parse_url. Preserve stock Haxe PHP implementation reuse, generated original-path shell manifests, PHP lint, and existing WPHX-312.42/WPHX-312.62-64 oracle/candidate probes.

Acceptance criteria: A compiler-emitted grouped original-path adapter covers at least one additional neighboring WP_Http parser/header helper beyond processResponse/chunkTransferDecode, or a minimized blocker documents the missing WPHX PHP feature; existing probes pass; manifests and progress notes distinguish generated public PHP from copied or JS-patched shells; whole-file WP_Http non-claims remain explicit.
close_reason: Extended grouped WPHX PHP original-path WP_Http parser helper emission to protected WP_Http::parse_url. The WPHX PHP emitter now honors @:wp.visibility on static methods; the grouped class-wp-http.php shell emits processResponse, chunkTransferDecode, and protected parse_url; the probe covers all three cases including subclass parent::parse_url access; manifests, compiler receipts, and progress docs were refreshed.

title: WPHX-306.07 — Installed auth distribution parity gate
description: Build the installed-distribution gate for the users/auth domain after the capability, cookie/nonce, password/application-password, and runtime ABI fixtures exist. The candidate must run through original-path generated PHP without oracle-source fallback for claimed auth surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Installed candidate distribution covers login/logout, auth cookies, nonces, user CRUD/profile flows, application passwords, role/capability checks, relevant admin screens, REST/AJAX auth surfaces, plugin-visible hooks, raw headers/cookies/errors, and selected upstream PHPUnit groups; no upstream public auth implementation masks claimed candidate behavior.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-306.07 installed-style auth distribution gate with package roots, PHP HTTP transport, cookie carry-over, package-boundary reflection, ownership manifest, receipt, evidence link, npm scripts, and progress-matrix update. The gate covers login/logout, auth Set-Cookie headers, logged-in profile access, AJAX nonce success/failure, REST-style application-password Basic auth, profile application-password creation, bad-password errors, and Haxe auth contract artifacts in the candidate package. Candidate public auth files remain copied oracle source, so generated public PHP replacement and full MySQL-backed installed WordPress parity are explicitly not claimed.

title: WPHX-306.06 — User/auth runtime ABI fixture
description: Build runtime reflection and plugin-facing ABI fixtures for WP_User, WP_Roles, WP_Role, WP_User_Query, WP_Session_Tokens, WP_User_Meta_Session_Tokens, application-password classes, public properties, dynamic properties, magic methods, declaring files/classes, serialization behavior, and get_object_vars ordering.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime ABI fixture records static declarations plus runtime reflection, declaring file/class, public property order, dynamic property behavior, serialization, references where observable, and plugin-authored probes against vanilla and candidate distributions; reports classify evidence scope and do not normalize away paths or declaring classes.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-306.06 user/auth runtime ABI fixture, ownership manifest, receipt, evidence link, npm check scripts, and progress-matrix update. The fixture covers WP_User, WP_Roles, WP_Role, WP_User_Query, WP_Session_Tokens, WP_User_Meta_Session_Tokens, and WP_Application_Passwords across reflection declarations, declaring files/classes, class attributes, public/default property order, method signatures, dynamic properties, magic methods, serialization shape, session token storage, and plugin-style object access. Candidate is still an oracle-source mirror; this does not claim generated public PHP replacement or installed login/auth parity.

title: WPHX-306.05 — Password and application-password oracle fixture

acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover phpass, bcrypt, WordPress bcrypt, unknown hash behavior, password rehash routes where observable, application-password authentication success/failure, hooks, errors, and relevant upstream PHPUnit coverage; no raw PHP body strings become production implementation.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-306.05 password/application-password oracle-source mirror fixture, ownership manifest, receipt, evidence link, npm check scripts, and progress-matrix update. Covers WordPress-prefixed bcrypt, vanilla bcrypt, legacy md5, too-long password behavior, password filters, application-password hashing/checking, app-password auth success/failure, usage recording, and basic-auth validation against separate oracle/candidate PHP source mirrors. This does not claim generated public PHP replacement, runtime ABI closure, or installed login/auth distribution parity.

title: WPHX-306.04 — Auth cookie and nonce oracle fixture
description: Build differential oracle fixtures for wp_set_auth_cookie, wp_validate_auth_cookie, wp_clear_auth_cookie, wp_create_nonce, wp_verify_nonce, session token lookup, deterministic salts, deterministic time ticks, raw headers/cookies, and pluggable declaration timing.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures capture raw cookie/header output, nonce return routes, session-token effects, warnings/notices, pluggable declaration timing, and relevant upstream auth/nonce PHPUnit coverage with explicit normalization only for documented nondeterminism.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-306.04 auth-cookie/nonce oracle-source mirror fixture and receipt. The fixture covers 9 cases and 11 symbols across wp_generate_auth_cookie, wp_parse_auth_cookie, wp_validate_auth_cookie, wp_set_auth_cookie, wp_clear_auth_cookie, wp_nonce_tick, wp_create_nonce, wp_verify_nonce, wp_hash/wp_salt, session-token lookup, and pluggable declaration timing. It records hook-level cookie intent and CLI header observations; real web-SAPI Set-Cookie parity remains for the installed distribution gate. This does not claim generated public PHP replacement.

title: WPHX-306.03 — Capability and role oracle fixture

acceptance_criteria: Capability and role fixtures cover primitive caps, meta caps, super-admin-sensitive caps, filters, missing users, role add/remove, and relevant upstream PHPUnit user/capability tests; reports and receipts distinguish oracle-source behavior from Haxe-owned adapter contracts.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-306.03 capability/role oracle-source mirror fixture and receipt. The fixture covers 9 cases and 21 symbols across WP_Role, WP_Roles, WP_User, map_meta_cap, current_user_can, user_can, role mutations, filters, missing users, super-admin behavior, and site-switch capability context. This is targeted semantic parity evidence for the auth domain; it does not claim generated public PHP replacement or installed auth distribution parity.

It does not claim Haxe runtime ownership yet.
WPHX-309.04 added the first typed Haxe routing/template adapter-contract candidate covering route classification, request-plan intent, canonical redirect intent, link-template routing, template-loader hierarchy intent, and rewrite/request/canonical/link/template hook intent. This remains helper-level semantic/adapter-contract evidence; no public PHP replacement, rewrite-rule storage, canonical redirect output, template include behavior, front-end HTTP parity, or installed-distribution parity is claimed.
WPHX-309.06 added the first deterministic oracle-source-mirror routing/link fixture covering selected WP_Rewrite tag/rule/permastruct state, home_url scheme/path handling, user_trailingslashit policy, and WP::parse_request matched rewrite, extra-query-var, and 404 observations. It is wired into deterministic CI and receipts. This is executable behavior evidence only; it does not claim Haxe-owned runtime replacement, public PHP replacement, full canonical redirect parity, template-loader include behavior, front-end HTTP parity, or installed-distribution parity.
close_reason: Closed bounded WPHX-309 rewrite/routing/canonical/template domain evidence slice across surface inventory, typed Haxe adapter-contract, routing/link and canonical/template oracle fixtures, installed HTTP gate, selected upstream group scope, provisioned pass/pass ratchet evidence, progress matrix, and domain closure receipt.

title: WPHX-308 — Taxonomy, terms, comments
description: Port and validate WordPress 7.0 taxonomy, terms, term metadata, comments, comment queries, counts, and moderation behavior after the WPHX-322 manifest split.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy/comment source units are mapped to Haxe-owned implementations or accepted adapter contracts; fixtures cover CRUD, hierarchy, counts, query behavior, cache invalidation, moderation hooks, and upstream PHPUnit/domain parity with receipts.
notes:
WPHX-308.01 established the taxonomy/terms/comments surface inventory: 36 classified source files, 36 distribution artifacts, 422 ABI entries, 7 classes, 80 upstream tests, cross-domain REST/query/block/admin handoff notes, and first adapter-contract fixture targets. It does not claim Haxe runtime ownership yet.
WPHX-308.02 added the first typed Haxe taxonomy/comment adapter-contract candidate covering taxonomy visibility/REST routing, term write/default-term routing, object-term relationship routing, count/cache invalidation intent, comment write/status routing, moderation routing, query filter classification, and hook intent. This remains helper-level adapter-contract evidence; no public PHP replacement, live DB parity, or installed taxonomy/comment behavior is claimed.
WPHX-308.03 added a deterministic PHP-authored oracle-source-mirror CRUD fixture for taxonomy registration, term insert/update/duplicate/delete, object-term assignment/append/replace/remove/count hooks, comment insert/update/approve/trash/delete, comment meta, cache cleanup, and transition hooks. This improves executable behavior evidence but still does not claim generated public PHP replacement, Haxe semantic ownership for CRUD, live DB parity, or installed taxonomy/comment behavior.
WPHX-308.04 added a deterministic oracle-source-mirror count/cache invalidation fixture for immediate and deferred term counts, clean_term_cache, clean_object_term_cache, immediate and deferred comment counts, clean_comment_cache, last_changed writes, cache deletes, option cleanup, post cache cleanup, and relevant hooks. It is wired into deterministic CI and receipts.
WPHX-308.05 added a deterministic oracle-source-mirror query-state fixture for selected WP_Term_Query and WP_Comment_Query query-var normalization, SQL request/clause construction, meta/date/search/include/order/count paths, and query hooks. It is wired into deterministic CI and receipts.
WPHX-308.06 added an initial live MySQL oracle-source-mirror SQL/result fixture for selected WP_Term_Query and WP_Comment_Query cases over real wpdb, separate oracle/candidate databases, termmeta/commentmeta joins, include/order/count paths, manifests, ownership, receipts, package scripts, and live DB CI wiring. Next WPHX-308 gates are selected upstream taxonomy/comment PHPUnit ratchets, MariaDB/PHP-version live DB expansion, and installed-distribution taxonomy/comment behavior.
WPHX-308.07 added selected upstream PHPUnit ratchet groups for taxonomy/term and comment core behavior: term CRUD/query/meta/cache/count/object relationships plus comment query/count/cache/moderation/date/slash behavior. The local ratchet report remains blocked until the disposable upstream PHPUnit runtime is provisioned, so this is selected upstream-suite coverage scope, not an executable upstream parity claim.
WPHX-308.08 expanded the taxonomy/comment live DB SQL/result fixture from the initial MySQL/PHP 8.4 lane to the pinned MySQL 8.4, MariaDB 11.8, PHP 8.4 DB-client, and PHP 8.5 DB-client matrix. The fixture now records four matching oracle/candidate comparisons over isolated databases. This remains oracle-source-mirror live integration evidence only; it does not claim Haxe-owned public PHP replacement, executable upstream PHPUnit closure, or installed taxonomy/comment behavior.
close_reason: Closed bounded WPHX-308 taxonomy/terms/comments domain evidence slice across surface inventory, Haxe adapter-contract, CRUD/count/cache/query/live-DB fixtures, selected upstream PHPUnit pass/pass evidence, installed-distribution gate, progress matrix, and domain closure receipt.

title: WPHX-317.07 — Promote first multisite pure helpers to Haxe parity candidates
description: After the WPHX-317 option/transient, blog-switching, and site/network object fixtures exist, extract the first safe multisite decision/model helpers into typed Haxe parity candidates without changing public PHP ABI.
acceptance_criteria: Typed Haxe candidates cover a small multisite helper set, avoid broad Dynamic/untyped/raw target code, preserve oracle behavior through WPHX-317 fixtures, and emit generated-shape plus behavior receipts.
notes: ADR-004 accepted 2026-06-22 after oracle review: WPHX-317.07 and later adapter work must treat PHP as the privileged compatibility host and Rust/native as future optional internal providers only. Track semantic_owner, adapter_contract_owner, emission_strategy, execution_provider, and compatibility_evidence separately. Haxe should own migrated behavior and adapter intent; upstream-derived or source-transformed PHP shells remain bridges until emitted from Haxe-owned adapter contracts. Native providers require pure value boundaries, PHP fallback, and native-on/native-off differential evidence.
close_reason: Added the first ADR-004-aware multisite Haxe semantic/adapter-contract candidate with typed Haxe ownership axes, generated PHP runtime checks across local/Docker PHP, no public PHP shell replacement claim, manifests, ownership manifest, receipt, npm scripts, and progress-matrix update.

description: Create the first deterministic multisite/network surface manifest for WPHX-317. Inventory WordPress 7.0 multisite source units, public PHP ABI symbols, upstream tests, first fixture targets, and deferred cross-domain boundaries for site options, site transients, sitemeta, network/site objects, bootstrap/routing, and network admin.
acceptance_criteria: A checked runner emits manifests/wp-core/wphx-317-01-multisite-network-surface.v1.json, manifests/ownership/wphx-317-01-multisite-network-surface.v1.json, and receipts/wp-core/wphx-317-01-multisite-network-surface.v1.json; package scripts run and check deterministically; Beads links the task under WPHX-317; validation and receipt checks pass.
close_reason: Generated deterministic WPHX-317.01 multisite/network surface, ownership manifest, receipt, package scripts, and follow-up WPHX-317 fixture/candidate tasks.

title: WPHX-311.14 — Port REST embed link decisions
description: Move WP_REST_Server::embed_links() branch decisions into typed Haxe strategy helpers while preserving native PHP data shape, WP_REST_Request::from_url(), dispatch(), rest_post_dispatch, embed cache values, and response_to_data() recursion in the PHP shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- RestServerDispatchStrategy owns typed embed_links branch helpers without Dynamic.
- Candidate class-wp-rest-server.php shell calls the Haxe strategy for embed_links() branch decisions.
- REST gates include an _embed request path and pass in packaged, web, and browser lanes.
- Remaining native PHP request parsing, dispatch, cache storage, and response recursion are documented as boundaries.
close_reason: Moved WP_REST_Server::embed_links branch decisions into typed Haxe helpers, added _embed coverage to packaged/web/browser gates, and preserved native PHP request parsing, dispatch, filters, cache, and response recursion boundaries.

title: WPHX-311.13 — Port REST response link target decisions
description: Move a bounded slice of WP_REST_Server::get_response_links() and get_target_hints_for_link() branch decisions into typed Haxe strategy helpers while preserving native PHP array shape, WP_REST_Request parsing, route matching, and allow-header extraction in the PHP shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- RestServerDispatchStrategy owns typed response-link and target-hint branch helpers without Dynamic.
- Candidate class-wp-rest-server.php shell calls the Haxe strategy for get_response_links() and get_target_hints_for_link() branch decisions.
- Existing packaged/web/browser REST gates pass and manifests/receipts reflect the new owned methods.
- Remaining native PHP array mutation, WP_REST_Request::from_url(), match_request_to_handler(), and rest_send_allow_header() behavior is documented as preserved boundary.
close_reason: Moved WP_REST_Server::get_response_links and get_target_hints_for_link branch decisions into typed Haxe helpers while preserving native PHP link arrays, request parsing, route matching, validation, sanitization, and allow-header boundaries; REST gates pass.

title: WPHX-311.12 — Port REST response data/link decisions
description: Move a bounded slice of WP_REST_Server response_to_data() and compact-response-link branch decisions into typed Haxe strategy helpers while preserving native PHP array/object operations and WordPress link/embed APIs in the public PHP shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- RestServerDispatchStrategy owns typed response data/link decision helpers without Dynamic.
- Candidate class-wp-rest-server.php shell calls the Haxe strategy for response_to_data() and get_compact_response_links() branch decisions.
- Existing packaged/web/browser REST gates pass and manifests/receipts reflect the new owned methods.
- Remaining native array mutation, embed_links(), get_response_links(), and target-hint behavior stay documented as PHP boundaries.
close_reason: Moved WP_REST_Server::response_to_data and get_compact_response_links branch decisions into typed Haxe helpers while preserving native PHP array/link/embed boundaries; REST package, web, browser, and dispatch gates pass.

title: WPHX-311.11 — Port REST serve_request decision pipeline
description: Move a bounded slice of WP_REST_Server::serve_request() branch decisions into typed Haxe strategy code while preserving PHP-native header/status/output/global side effects in the public PHP shell. Use existing packaged, web-server, and browser gates as the behavior authority.
acceptance_criteria:
- RestServerDispatchStrategy owns typed serve_request decision helpers without Dynamic.
- Candidate class-wp-rest-server.php shell calls the Haxe strategy for serve_request branch decisions.
- Existing packaged/web/browser REST gates pass and receipts/manifests reflect serve_request strategy ownership.
- Remaining PHP-native effects and unported REST server bodies are explicitly documented as boundaries/follow-ups.
close_reason: Moved WP_REST_Server::serve_request branch decisions into typed Haxe strategy helpers while preserving PHP-native WordPress side effects in the shell; packaged, web, browser, and dispatch gates pass.

title: WPHX-311.10 — Add DB-backed and cross-origin REST browser matrix
description: Follow WPHX-311.09 with a database-backed installed WordPress REST browser matrix and a cross-origin CORS/preflight harness. Keep vanilla and candidate roots isolated, classify evidence separately from deterministic fixture gates, and preserve candidate no-fallback assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Database-backed installed REST roots compare vanilla and candidate for representative /wp-json/wp/v2/settings and REST server cases.
- Cross-origin browser fetch/preflight cases capture Access-Control behavior, exposed headers, allowed headers/methods, status, and body.
- Matrix placement is cost-tiered between PR and nightly with manifests/receipts.
- Any known candidate deltas are recorded with owner and expiry instead of broad skips.
close_reason: Added DB-backed installed REST browser matrix with isolated MySQL-backed oracle/candidate roots, browser same-origin fetches, cross-origin CORS/preflight coverage, package no-fallback assertions, PR live-DB CI wiring, manifests, ownership, and receipt.

title: WPHX-311.09 — Add installed/browser REST E2E corpus
description: Follow the WPHX-311.08 PHP built-in web-server gate with installed-distribution REST coverage that routes through WordPress entry points and browser/fetch automation. Keep vanilla/candidate comparisons separated and preserve package no-fallback assertions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Installed distribution or index.php/rest entry routing compares vanilla and candidate for representative /wp-json/wp/v2/settings and REST server cases.
- Browser/fetch automation captures status, selected headers, body, CORS/preflight where relevant, and permission/filter error paths.
- The gate is labeled with evidence_class/artifact_scope and wired into CI/nightly at the appropriate cost tier.
- Remaining REST server method-body ownership gaps are updated with receipts or follow-up beads.
close_reason: Implemented installed-style index.php REST routing and Playwright/browser fetch parity gate with CI wiring, manifests, receipts, and follow-up WPHX-311.10.

title: WPHX-311.08 — Add REST web-server E2E and upstream PHPUnit ratchet
description: Follow the WPHX-311.07 CLI packaged HTTP gate with real installed/web-server REST coverage and upstream REST PHPUnit ratcheting. Cover transport-level headers, routing through index/rest entry points, browser/fetch behavior where relevant, and classify vanilla/candidate PHPUnit results without weakening the upstream suite.
acceptance_criteria: A real web-server or installed-distribution REST gate compares vanilla and candidate behavior for /wp-json/wp/v2/settings success/error/filter cases; transport headers/status/body behavior is captured outside CLI; affected upstream REST PHPUnit groups run against vanilla and candidate with known-deltas classification; manifests, receipts, CI/nightly placement, and remaining REST ownership gaps are updated.
close_reason: Implemented REST web-server E2E gate, upstream REST PHPUnit ratchet group declarations, CI wiring, manifests, receipts, and follow-up WPHX-311.09.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: CI hardening follow-up: the Node-side REST web E2E runner now retries transient fetch exceptions before classifying behavior. This mirrors the existing browser-context retry and preserves HTTP status/body parity checks; it is a transport-stability fix, not a semantic normalization.

title: WPHX-311.07 — Add packaged REST server distribution and HTTP serving gate
description: Follow WPHX-311.06 by proving the REST server dispatch candidate in an assembled distribution boundary: replace/own the relevant WP_REST_Server surface without upstream fallback masking, then cover serve_request()/HTTP response behavior, headers/CORS/body parsing/JSON emission, and plugin-facing filters against the locked WordPress oracle.
acceptance_criteria: A packaged distribution gate proves the REST server candidate owns the relevant declaring files/classes/methods without upstream fallback; serve_request()/HTTP-level REST behavior compares against vanilla WordPress for settings endpoint success/error/filter cases; manifests, ownership, receipts, CI gating, and remaining upstream PHPUnit/E2E boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Added WPHX-311.07 packaged REST server HTTP gate. The runner builds oracle and candidate package roots, compiles the Haxe dispatch strategy into the candidate package, proves WP_REST_Server serve_request/dispatch/respond_to_request are declared from the package class file without upstream class fallback across local PHP and pinned Docker runtimes, and compares CLI-safe serve_request() output/status/header-intent traces for settings success, update, no-route, HEAD, and rest_pre_serve_request manual serving cases. Added manifests, ownership, receipt, package scripts, and required CI gate. Filed WPHX-311.08 for real web-server E2E and upstream REST PHPUnit ratcheting.

title: WPHX-311.06 — Promote REST server dispatch routing to Haxe candidate
description: Move a bounded WP_REST_Server dispatch routing slice into typed Haxe after the REST settings controller strategy candidates: route match result handling, permission callback result routing, WP_Error response conversion decision, rest_pre_dispatch/rest_request_before_callbacks/rest_dispatch_request short-circuit routing, and matched route/handler metadata preservation.
acceptance_criteria: A typed Haxe REST server dispatch strategy is added without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or broad php.Syntax.code; the PHP server shell preserves public WP_REST_Server ABI and plugin filter/callback behavior; oracle/candidate parity covers the WPHX-311.02 dispatch cases across local PHP and pinned PHP runtimes; WPHX-311.01-.05 checks continue to pass; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining HTTP-serving/distribution boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Added typed Haxe REST server dispatch strategy candidate for WP_REST_Server::dispatch and respond_to_request, preserving PHP plugin/filter ABI through a linked candidate shell. Oracle/candidate parity covers the WPHX-311.02 dispatch cases across local PHP and pinned Docker PHP runtimes; WPHX-311.01-.05 checks continue to pass; manifests, ownership, receipts, escape-hatch audit, and CI gate are updated. Filed WPHX-311.07 for packaged distribution and HTTP serving closure.

title: WPHX-311.05 — Promote REST settings update-item routing to Haxe candidate
description: Move WP_REST_Settings_Controller update_item routing decisions into typed Haxe: request param presence, rest_pre_update_setting short-circuit, null delete routing, invalid stored value rejection, and update_option routing while preserving PHP request/array/filter ABI.
acceptance_criteria: A typed Haxe update-item strategy is added without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or broad php.Syntax.code; the PHP controller shell preserves public ABI and native WordPress filter/storage/schema boundaries; oracle/candidate parity covers update, null delete, pre-update override, invalid stored value error, and response refresh across local PHP and pinned PHP runtimes; WPHX-311.01-.04 checks continue to pass; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Added typed Haxe REST settings update_item strategy candidate with normal update, missing-param skip, pre-update short-circuit, null delete, invalid stored-value error parity, receipts, and CI gate.

title: WPHX-311.04 — Promote REST settings get-item value strategy to Haxe candidate
description: Move the next bounded WP_REST_Settings_Controller slice into typed Haxe: get_item value preparation decisions around rest_pre_get_setting fallback, invalid schema values becoming null, and non-null values flowing through native REST sanitization while preserving the public PHP controller ABI.
acceptance_criteria: A typed Haxe REST settings value strategy is added without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or broad php.Syntax.code; the candidate PHP shell preserves WordPress-facing get_item/prepare_value ABI and native filter/schema boundaries; oracle/candidate parity passes across local PHP and pinned PHP runtimes; WPHX-311.01/.02/.03 checks continue to pass; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Added typed Haxe REST settings value strategy candidate for get_item/prepare_value with oracle parity, CI gate, receipts, and explicit update_item follow-up gap.

title: WPHX-311.02 — Add REST settings server-dispatch fixture
description: Extend WPHX-311 beyond direct WP_REST_Settings_Controller method calls by exercising WP_REST_Server dispatch for /wp/v2/settings, including request validation, permission failures, WP_Error-to-response conversion, response status/data shape, and relevant authentication/filter boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic fixture compares oracle and candidate behavior for REST server dispatch of /wp/v2/settings across local PHP and pinned PHP runtimes; route arg validation/sanitization, permission-denied responses, WP_Error conversion, and response data/status are covered; manifests, ownership, receipts, and CI gates are updated.
close_reason: Added REST settings server-dispatch fixture with oracle/candidate parity, receipts, ownership manifest, and required CI gate.

title: WPHX-311.03 — Promote REST settings strategy to Haxe candidate
description: Use the WPHX-311.01 fixture to move the first bounded WP_REST_Settings_Controller decisions into typed Haxe, starting with registered option filtering/schema planning, permission plan, value preparation/null handling, or update routing where public PHP ABI remains preserved.
acceptance_criteria: A selected REST settings-controller decision slice is implemented in typed Haxe without unjustified Dynamic, untyped, casts, or broad php.Syntax.code; generated PHP preserves WordPress-facing ABI and idiom; WPHX-311.01 and any dispatch fixture continue to pass; manifests, ownership, receipts, and remaining native boundaries are updated.
close_reason: Added typed Haxe REST settings schema strategy candidate with oracle/candidate parity, CI gate, receipts, and explicit remaining REST boundaries.

title: WPHX-311.01 — Build REST settings controller fixture harness
description: Build the first WPHX-311 differential fixture around WP_REST_Settings_Controller, covering show_in_rest schema emission, route registration, permission callback behavior, get/update/null-error flows, and option storage through a deterministic wpdb/cache test double before typed Haxe REST ownership.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic runner compares oracle and candidate WP_REST_Settings_Controller behavior against the locked WordPress 7.0 source across local PHP and pinned PHP runtimes; manifests, ownership, and receipt record REST/native boundaries and remaining Haxe candidate gaps; package scripts and validation checks pass.
close_reason: Implemented WPHX-311.01 REST settings-controller differential fixture. The runner mirrors the locked WordPress 7.0 REST/settings/options source into oracle and candidate roots, compares route registration, permission callback, show_in_rest schema emission, get/update/null-error flows, sanitize_callback behavior, deterministic wpdb option storage, and REST schema validation across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Added manifest, ownership, receipt, package scripts, and required CI deterministic parity gate. Verified npm run wp:core:wphx-311-rest-settings-controller:check, npm run ci:php-conformance:check, npm run beads:validate, and npm run receipts:validate.

title: WPHX-700.07 — Expand generated-PHP lowering snapshot cases
description: Extend the WPHX-700.02 generated-PHP lowering snapshot lane beyond the initial mysqli @:phpGlobal minimized fixture. Add cases for by-reference parameters/returns/globals/array elements, public class shape, include/caller-scope semantics, conditional declarations/global functions, and native PHP array/object/resource boundary values as those surfaces become active in the WordPress port.
acceptance_criteria: Each added lowering case has an explicit case id, evidence_class=generated_shape, artifact_scope=minimized_fixture or linked_candidate as appropriate, compile-twice determinism, php -l, selected exact golden where small enough, AST-normalized contract for the relevant PHP shape, and no claim of WordPress behavior parity.
close_reason: Expanded generated-PHP lowering snapshots with WPHX-700.07 public-class and native object/array boundary minimized fixtures, exact goldens, AST contracts, compile-twice determinism, PHP lint, and dedicated manifest/receipt evidence.

title: WPHX-700.06 — Centralize parity runner reports with evidence metadata
description: Create shared test-runner/report infrastructure so WPHX runners do not each reimplement compilation, Docker startup, hashing, normalization, stale-file checks, and receipt metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Shared support utilities emit complete JSON reports and optional JUnit, include evidence_class and artifact_scope, preserve full stdout/stderr artifacts by digest, support deterministic check mode, and are adopted by at least one existing WPHX runner without changing its behavior.
close_reason: Added shared runner support utilities, deterministic support checker, docs, and adopted them in the upstream PHPUnit ratchet runner.

title: WPHX-305.09 — Add locked PHP DB-client image for live DB gates
description: Build or select a locked PHP CLI runtime image that includes mysqli and pdo_mysql so live database gates can run PHP-version client matrices instead of relying only on the host PHP mysqli extension. WPHX-305.07 records this as a remaining native runtime boundary because the official locked PHP CLI images lack those extensions.
acceptance_criteria: Toolchain lock records reproducible image digests for PHP DB-client runtimes; WPHX-305.07 or its successor can run the live MySQL/MariaDB gate through the locked PHP DB-client image matrix; manifests and receipts distinguish database server runtime coverage from PHP client runtime coverage; secret scanning and precommit gates pass.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-305.09: locked PHP DB-client image inputs, verifier, and live DB matrix integration.

title: WPHX-322 — PHP first-party manifest closure
description: Close the WordPress PHP first-party manifest after WPHX-301 through WPHX-320 source-unit domains have parity receipts. This owns the full upstream PHPUnit/domain closure gaps recorded by WPHX-304 receipts.
acceptance_criteria: All C1 first-party PHP source/artifact entries are verified or explicitly split with receipts; upstream PHPUnit/domain parity gaps recorded by WPHX-304 are resolved or linked to accepted follow-up ownership; final closure receipts validate the complete PHP first-party manifest.
close_reason: Added deterministic WPHX-322 first-party PHP manifest closure/split runner and receipt. The manifest assigns 1,564 C1 WordPress 7.0 PHP runtime source entries and 1,222 C1 PHP distribution artifacts to closed receipt-backed domains or explicit WPHX-306 through WPHX-320 follow-up owners, with zero unassigned; PHP vendor entries are excluded to WPHX-323. This is manifest ownership/split closure, not a broad Haxe implementation claim.

title: WPHX-317 — Multisite and network
description: Port and validate multisite/network behavior, including the site option and site transient sitemeta paths deferred by WPHX-304.03 and WPHX-304.06.
acceptance_criteria: Multisite install/routing/site/network/user-admin fixtures compare against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle; site option, site transient, cache group, and sitemeta storage paths are covered; ownership manifests and receipts identify single-site versus multisite boundaries.
close_reason: Added WPHX-317 domain closure receipt covering all seven child slices, linked evidence, updated progress matrix, and verified the WPHX-317 check set. Closure is bounded to targeted oracle/candidate fixtures plus the first Haxe multisite adapter-contract candidate; public PHP replacement and installed distribution claims remain later gates.

title: WPHX-311 — REST API and schema
description: Port and validate WordPress REST API and schema behavior, including the show_in_rest settings registry bridge surfaced by WPHX-304.06. This remains deferred until the prerequisite PHP core domains are in place.
acceptance_criteria: REST endpoint, schema, auth/error, and settings-controller fixtures compare against the locked WordPress 7.0 oracle; show_in_rest settings schema behavior from WPHX-304.06 is covered through WP_REST_Settings_Controller; ownership manifests and receipts record REST/native boundaries.
close_reason: Closed REST API/schema domain after attaching WPHX-311.09 through WPHX-311.14 to the parent graph and recording domain closure evidence for settings, server dispatch, typed strategy candidates, packaged HTTP, installed browser, DB-backed browser, and cross-origin CORS/preflight gates.

title: WPHX-210 — Build profiles: debug, parity, release
description: Define deterministic build profiles for developer debugging, parity validation, and release artifact generation.
acceptance_criteria: Profile outputs are deterministic and documented, with intentional differences recorded.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-210 build profiles: npm run build:profiles:check and npm run ci:php-conformance:check pass; profiles/wp70-build-profiles.v1.json defines deterministic wp70-debug, wp70-parity, and wp70-release contracts with original-path linker policy preserved and intentional differences recorded. Receipt: receipts/build-profiles/wphx-210-build-profiles.v1.json.

title: WPHX-207 — Debug/source-map/error-path support
description: Preserve useful traces across generated PHP, Haxe source, and original WordPress source units.
acceptance_criteria: Traces map to Haxe and original source units.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-207 debug/source-map/error-path support: npm run wp:debug:sourcemap:check passes; runtime exception traces map from generated Haxe PHP to fixture Haxe source and from generated original-path shell frames/markers to source:wordpress-7.0.0:src/wp-includes/load.php across local PHP plus pinned Docker PHP 8.4/8.5. Receipt: receipts/wp-debug/wphx-207-source-map-traces.v1.json.

title: WPHX-016 — Evaluate Beads CLI upgrade from 0.49.0
description: bd doctor reports 1.0.4 available while this repo is pinned to 0.49.0. Evaluate upgrade impact on hooks, JSONL sync, merge driver, and AGENTS workflow before changing the pin.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded; if upgraded, .beads/.local_version, toolchain.lock.json, docs/operations/beads.md, hooks, and a clean bd doctor receipt are updated together.
close_reason: Verified Beads upgrade to stable 1.0.4, embedded Dolt bootstrap from JSONL, hook compatibility updates, and backup/dolt command availability for WPHX-807.

title: WPHX-011 — License/provenance audit
acceptance_criteria: Licenses, notices, and vendor origins are recorded.
close_reason: Recorded WPHX-011 license/provenance audit with deterministic manifest, runbook, package-level exceptions, and receipt.

title: WPHX-803 — Implement deterministic PRD-key to Beads-issue seeding
acceptance_criteria: Stable WPHX external refs import/update idempotently.
notes: Implemented deterministic Beads seed manifest and idempotent seed checker/apply tooling keyed by WPHX external refs. Commands passed: node tools/beads/export-seed-manifest.mjs, npm run beads:seed, npm run beads:seed:apply with zero changes, npm run beads:seed. Receipt: receipts/operations/wphx-803-prd-seed.v1.json.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-803 deterministic WPHX-key Beads seeding.

title: WPHX-804 — Generate bounded task packets from Beads and manifests
acceptance_criteria: One command emits reproducible context for a selected issue.
notes: Implemented bounded task packet generator. Command npm run task-pack -- WPHX-009 --out build/task-packs/WPHX-009.json produced deterministic sample packet sha256 875946ddce65d0613c0e64d4e30592acd224074867438b47a54f193174557b12. npm run task-pack:check passed. Receipt: receipts/operations/wphx-804-task-pack.v1.json.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-804 deterministic task packet generator.

title: WPHX-805 — Link verification receipts and manifest digests to Beads issues
acceptance_criteria: Close validation rejects missing or stale evidence.
notes: Implemented receipt evidence-link manifest and validator. Added retrospective receipts for WPHX-001, WPHX-014, and WPHX-801 so closed tasks have explicit evidence. npm run receipts:build and npm run receipts:validate passed. Receipt: receipts/operations/wphx-805-receipt-links.v1.json.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-805 receipt evidence-link and stale digest validation.

title: WPHX-806 — Prove multi-agent claim/worktree/handoff protocol
acceptance_criteria: Two-agent drill has no duplicate claims, path collisions, or lost discoveries.
close_reason: Verified WPHX-806 multi-agent claim/worktree/handoff drill with root Beads wrapper, non-overlap validator, task packets, and receipt.

title: WPHX-807 — Configure Dolt sync plus bd backup and perform restore drill
acceptance_criteria: Fresh clone/restore reproduces tasks, dependencies, memory, and ready queue.
close_reason: Verified Dolt remote sync, filesystem bd backup sync, fresh clone bootstrap, backup restore, dependency edge match, memory surface match, and ready queue match.

title: WPHX-009 — Create initial parity dashboard
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard can query by file, API, package, task, and gate.
notes: Created initial parity dashboard CLI and generated indexes. Commands: npm run dashboard:build, npm run dashboard -- <summary|file|api|package|task|gate>, npm run dashboard:check. Query checks passed for file wp-includes/plugin.php, API add_filter, package block-editor, task WPHX-009, and gate G0. Receipt: receipts/dashboard/wphx-009-parity-dashboard.v1.json.
close_reason: Completed WPHX-009 initial parity dashboard queries for file, API, package, task, and gate.

title: WPHX-700 — Parity, security, performance, and distribution
WPHX-MTGT — Evaluate future multi-target WordPressHX profiles
description: Track the later-stage research lane for compiling Haxe-owned WordPress semantics to alternate targets such as WASM via back2dos/wasmix, Go via fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.go, and Rust via fullofcaffeine/reflaxe.rust. This is not current PHP parity scope. It should start only after PHP-hosted compatibility and browser/runtime boundary evidence can define explicit compatibility profiles, including whether third-party PHP plugin/theme support is provided through generated PHP adapters, embedded PHP, or a narrower non-plugin profile.
acceptance_criteria: A future ADR or task pack defines bounded compatibility profiles, target candidates, PHP plugin/theme adapter story, evidence gates, and non-goals before implementation starts.
